questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: $$\vec{E}$$ and $$\vec{B}$$ represent the electric and the magnetic field of an electro- magnetic wave respectively. The direction of propagation of the wave is along
questions.solution:
The direction of propagation of an electromagnetic wave is given by the cross product of the electric field vector ($$\vec{E}$$) and the magnetic field vector ($$\vec{B}$$). Mathematically, this is represented as :

$$ \vec{E} \times \vec{B} $$

Hence, the direction of propagation of the wave is along Option C : $$\vec{E} \times \vec{B}$$.

In an electromagnetic wave, the electric field ($$\vec{E}$$), the magnetic field ($$\vec{B}$$), and the direction of propagation are all mutually perpendicular to each other. This relationship ensures that the wave propagates in a direction that is orthogonal to both the electric and magnetic fields.

To summarize, for an electromagnetic wave :

$$\vec{E} \perp \vec{B}$$

The wave propagation direction is given by $$\vec{E} \times \vec{B}$$

Thus, the correct answer is :

Option C

$$ \vec{E} \times \vec{B} $$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
(a) How are infrared waves produced? Why are these waves referred to as heat waves? Give any two uses of infrared waves.

OR

(b) How are X-rays produced? Give any two uses of these.
questions.solution:
(a) Production of infrared waves :

$$\bullet$$ Any object having temperature emits infrared (even ice emits infrared).

$$\bullet$$ Sun is a source of infrared.

$$\bullet$$ Infrared is produced by Nernst lamp, infrared laser etc.

Infrared waves are heat waves :

$$\bullet$$ It increases the vibration of atoms of the substances on which it falls and thus increases the internal energy and temperature of the substance.

Uses of infrared :

$$\bullet$$ It is used for photography at night and through fog.

$$\bullet$$ It is used for relieve of muscular pain.

OR

(b) Production of X-rays :

$$\bullet$$ X-rays are produced when high energy electron beam bombards metallic target of high melting and point heavy atomic weight.

$$\bullet$$ $$\mathrm{X}$$-ray is produced in Coolidge tube.

Uses of X-ray :

$$\bullet$$ It is used to photograph bones for the diagnosis of any fracture and dislocation.

$$\bullet$$ It is used to radiotherapy to destroy cancerous cells and to shrink tumors.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
The electric field associated with an electromagnetic wave propagating in a dielectric medium is given by $\vec{E}=30(2 \hat{x}+\hat{y}) \sin \left[2 \pi\left(5 \times 10^{14} t-\frac{10^7}{3} z\right)\right] \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$. Which of the following option(s) is(are) correct?

[Given: The speed of light in vacuum, $c=3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ ]
options: The wave is polarized in the $x y$-plane with polarization angle $30^{\circ}$ with respect to the $x$-axis.
The refractive index of the medium is 2.
questions.solution:
1. Calculation of the Speed of Light in a Medium:

- The speed of light in a medium, denoted as $$ V $$, is given by the formula $$ V = \frac{W}{k} $$.

- Substituting values, we get $$ V = \frac{3 \times 5 \times 10^{14}}{10^7} = 1.5 \times 10^8 \text{ m/s} $$.

2. Determination of the Refractive Index:

- The refractive index, $$ \mu $$, is the ratio of the speed of light in vacuum, $$ C $$, to the speed of light in the medium, $$ V $$.

- Substituting values, $$ \mu = \frac{3 \times 10^8}{1.5 \times 10^8} = 2 $$.

- Hence, the refractive index $$ \mu $$ is 2.

Therefore, the option (D) is the answer.

Given, $\vec{E}=30(2 \hat{x}+\hat{y}) \sin \left[2 \pi\left(5 \times 10^{14} t-\frac{10^7}{3} z\right)\right] \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$

Now magnitude of

$$ \begin{aligned} B & =\frac{E}{v} \\\\ \Rightarrow B & =\frac{30 \sqrt{5}}{1.5 \times 10^8} \end{aligned} $$



$\begin{aligned} & \tan \alpha=\frac{1}{2} \\\\ & \sin \alpha=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\end{aligned}$

The direction of $\vec{B}$ is along the direction of $\vec{v} \times \vec{E}$

i.e. $\hat{v} \times \hat{E}=\hat{B}$

Now $ B_x=-B \sin \alpha$

$$ \Rightarrow B_x=-\frac{30 \sqrt{5}}{1.5 \times 10^8} \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}=-2 \times 10^7 $$

Therefore, option (A) is the answer.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: During the formation of a $$p$$-$$n$$ junction:
options:
diffusion current keeps increasing.
drift current remains constant.
both the diffusion current and drift current remain constant.
diffusion current remains almost constant but drift current increases till both currents become equal.
questions.solution:
The formation of a p-n junction in semiconductor physics involves the interaction of p-type and n-type materials, leading to the establishment of diffusion and drift currents. When a p-n junction is formed:

  1. Diffusion Current: This is caused by the movement of charge carriers (holes from the p-side and electrons from the n-side) across the junction due to the concentration gradient. Initially, this diffusion current is high because of the large concentration difference between the p and n sides.


  2. Drift Current: This is caused by the electric field established at the junction due to the diffusion of charge carriers. This electric field opposes the diffusion current by driving the carriers back to their respective sides.

As the junction reaches equilibrium, the diffusion current decreases because the concentration gradient reduces. Simultaneously, the drift current increases as more immobile ions are exposed at the junction, strengthening the electric field. Eventually, these two currents balance each other, and the net current becomes zero.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option D: Diffusion current remains almost constant but drift current increases till both currents become equal.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
Assertion (A) : The resistance of an intrinsic semiconductor decreases with increase in its temperature.

Reason (R) : The number of conduction electrons as well as hole increase in an intrinsic semiconductor with rise in its temperature.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
Assertion (A) is true and Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false and Reason (R) is also false.
questions.solution:
In semiconductors, as temperature increases, thermally generated electron and hole pair increases and the resistance decreases.

Hence, assertion and reason both are correct and the reason explains the assertion.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
(a) Draw the circuit arrangement for studying V-I characteristics of a $$p$$-$$n$$ junction diode in (i) forward biasing and (ii) reverse biasing. Draw the typical V-I characteristics of a silicon diode. Describe briefly the following terms: (i) minority carrier injection in forward biasing and (ii) breakdown voltage in reverse biasing.

OR

(b) Name two important processes involved in the formation of a p-n junction diode. With the help of a circuit diagram, explain the working of junction diode as a full wave rectifier. Draw its input and output waveforms. State the characteristic property of a junction diode that makes it suitable for rectification.
questions.solution:
(a) Circuit diagram to study the V-I Characteristics of $$p$$-$$n$$ junction diode in

(i) Forward biasing:

(ii) Reverse biasing:

V-I Characteristics of Diode

(i) Minority carrier injection in forward bias:

In forward biased p-n junction, electrons from n-region diffuse into p-region where they are minority carriers. Holes are injected from p-side to n-side where they are minority carriers. This process is known as 'minority carrier injection'.

(ii) Breakdown voltage in reverse bias: In reverse biased p-n junction, only minority carriers can cross the junction. Hence, a very small amount of current flows in reverse direction which is known as reverse saturation current. If the reverse voltage is increased to a very large value, a large reverse current flows through the diode due to zener diode breakdown. The voltage at which this happens is known as breakdown voltage.

OR

(b) Two processes involved in formation of p-n junction:

(i) Diffusion

(ii) Drift

Full wave rectifier:

A centre-tapped transformer and two $$p$$-$$n$$ junction diodes are used for a full weave rectifier.

Input of the transformer is connected to the ac supply.

In secondary there are three terminals - A, B and CT. For positive half cycle, A is positive, B is negative. For the negative half cycle, A is negative, B is positive. CT is always at zero potential. It is always grounded. Anode of one diode $$\left(D_1\right)$$ is connected to A and anode of other diode $$\left(\mathrm{D}_2\right)$$ is connected to $$\mathrm{B}$$.

Cathodes of both the diodes are joined together and ultimately connected to CT through a load resistance $$\left(\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{L}}\right)$$.

When positive half cycle appears, $$\mathrm{A}$$ is at positive and $B$ is at negative potential. So, diode $$D_1$$ is forward biased and hence, it conducts. When negative half cycle appears, B is at positive and $$A$$ is at negative potential. So, diode $$D_2$$ is forward biased and hence, it conducts.

The process repeats.

For both the half cycles current flowing through the load resistance is unidirectional. Hence, a DC voltage appears across it. Thus, a full wave rectifier works.

Input and output wave forms:

Characteristic property of junction diode that makes it suitable for rectification:

An ideal $$p$$-$$n$$ junction diode exhibits zero resistance when forward biased and infinite resistance when reversed biased.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: In an extrinsic semiconductor, the number density of holes is $$4 \times 10^{20} \mathrm{~m}^{-3}$$. If the number density of intrinsic carriers is $$1.2 \times 10^{15} \mathbf{m}^3$$, the number density of electrons in it is
questions.solution:
From law of mass action,

$$\begin{aligned} n_i^2 & =n_e \times n_h \\ \text{or,} \quad \left(1.2 \times 10^{15}\right)^2 & =n_e \times 4 \times 10^{20} \\ \text{or,} \quad n_e & =(0.3 \times 1.2) \times 10^{10} \\ \therefore \quad n_e & =3.6 \times 10^9 \mathrm{~m}^{-3} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: Pieces of copper and of silicon are initially at room temperature. Both are heated to temperature T. The conductivity of
options:
both increases.
both decreases.
copper increases and silicon decreases.
copper decreases and silicon increases.
questions.solution: Copper is a conductor. As the temperature of copper increases the ions vibrate vigorously. So, the number of collisions between the free electrons and the ions increases. Hence, the resistance increases and conductivity decreases. Silicon is a semiconductor. As the temperature increases, more covalent bonds rupture generating more free charge carriers. Hence, the resistance decreases and conductivity increases.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: The formation of depletion region in a $$p$$-$$n$$ junction diode is due to
options:
movement of dopant atoms
diffusion of both electrons and holes
drift of electrons only
drift of holes only
questions.solution: During formation of p-n junction, the electrons diffuse from $$n$$-side to p-side (since, electron concentration is more in $$\mathrm{n}$$-side compared to $$\mathrm{p}$$-side) and the holes diffuse from $$p$$-side to $$n$$-side (since, hole concentration is more in p-side compared to n-side).

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: Draw energy band diagram for an $$n$$-type and $$p$$-type semiconductor at $$\mathrm{T}>0 \mathrm{~K}$$.

questions.question:
Answer the following giving reasons:

(i) A $$p$$-$$n$$ junction diode is damaged by a strong current.

(ii) Impurities are added in intrinsic semiconductors.
questions.solution:
(a) A p-n junction diode is damaged by strong current due to generation of heat.

(b) Impurities are added to intrinsic semiconductor to convert it to extrinsic semiconductor so that they achieve conductivity even at $$0 \mathrm{~K}$$.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: An electric dipole of length $$2 \mathrm{~cm}$$ is placed at an angle of $$30^{\circ}$$ with an electric field $$2 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C}$$. If the dipole experiences a torque of $$8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~Nm}$$, the magnitude of either charge of the dipole, is
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} \theta & =30^{\circ} \\ E & =2 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} \tau & =8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{Nm} \\ l & =2 \mathrm{~cm}=2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m} \\ \tau & =p E \sin \theta \end{aligned}\\ &[p=q l] \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{array}{rlrl} \therefore & \tau =q l E \sin \theta \\ \text { Or, } & q =\frac{\tau}{l E \sin \theta} \\ \text { Or, } & q =\frac{8 \times 10^{-3}}{2 \times 10^{-2} \times 2 \times 10^5 \times \sin 30^{\circ}} \\ \therefore & q =4 \mu \mathrm{C} \end{array}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: Depict the orientation of an electric dipole in (a) stable and (b) unstable equilibrium in an external uniform electric field. Write the potential energy of the dipole in each case.
questions.solution:
(a) Stable equilibrium: When angle between $$\vec{p}$$ and $$\vec{E}$$ is $$0^0$$.

Potential energy of dipole $$=-p E \cos \theta$$

In this case $$\theta=0^0$$, so Potential energy $$=-p \mathrm{E}$$

(b) Unstable equilibrium: When angle between $$\vec{p}$$ and $$\vec{E}$$ is $$180^{\circ}$$.

Potential energy of dipole $$=-p E \cos \theta$$

In this case $$\theta=180^{\circ}$$, so Potential energy $$=p \mathrm{E}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: The magnitude of the electric field due to a point charge object at a distance of $$4.0 \mathrm{~m}$$ is $$9 \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{C}}$$. From the same charged object the electric field of magnitude, $$16 \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{C}}$$ will be at a distance of
questions.solution:
The magnitude of the electric field due to a point charge is given by:

$$ E = \frac{k \cdot q}{r^2} $$

where:

$ E $ is the electric field,

$ k $ is Coulomb's constant ($8.99 \times 10^9 \, \frac{\mathrm{Nm}^2}{\mathrm{C}^2}$),

$ q $ is the charge,

$ r $ is the distance from the charge.

Given:

The electric field $ E_1 = 9 \, \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{C}} $ at $ r_1 = 4.0 \, \mathrm{m} $.

The electric field $ E_2 = 16 \, \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{C}} $ and we need to find the corresponding distance $ r_2 $.

Using the inverse square law relationship for electric fields from a point charge:

$$ \frac{E_1}{E_2} = \frac{r_2^2}{r_1^2} $$

Substituting the known values:

$$ \frac{9}{16} = \frac{r_2^2}{(4.0)^2} $$

which simplifies to:

$$ \frac{9}{16} = \frac{r_2^2}{16} $$

By cross-multiplying and solving for $ r_2 $:

$$ 9 \cdot 16 = 16 \cdot r_2^2 $$

$$ 9 = r_2^2 $$

Finally, taking the square root of both sides:

$$ r_2 = \sqrt{9} = 3 \, \mathrm{m} $$

Thus, the distance at which the electric field of magnitude $ 16 \, \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{C}} $ will be:

Option C:

$$3 \, \mathrm{m}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: A point $$P$$ lies at a distance $$x$$ from the mid point of an electric dipole on its axis. The electric potential at point $$\mathrm{P}$$ is proportional to
questions.solution:
Expression for electric potential due to dipole in given as $$V=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{p}{x^2}$$

So, $$V \propto \frac{1}{x^2}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: Beams of electrons and protons move parallel to each other in the same direction. They
options:
attract each other.
repel each other.
neither attract nor repel.
force of attraction or repulsion depends upon speed of beams.
questions.solution: Since, electron and proton has opposite charge and moving in the same direction, So there must be an attractive force between them.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
Assertion (A) : Work done in moving a charge around a closed path, in an electric field is always zero.

Reason (R) : Electrostatic force is a conservative force.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).

Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) and true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.solution:
Electric field is said to be conservative since, work done in this field is path independent. It depends on the end points only. So, in an electric field, total work done in moving a charge from a point to the next point and again back to the first point is zero.

Hence, assertion and reason both are true and reason explains the assertion.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
(a) (i) Use Gauss' law to obtain an expression for the electric field due to an infinitely long thin straight wire with uniform linear charge density $$\lambda$$.

(ii) An infinitely long positively charged straight wire has a linear charge density $$\lambda$$. An electron is revolving in a circle with a constant speed $$v$$ such that the wire passes through the centre, and is perpendicular to the plane, of the circle. Find the kinetic energy of the electron in terms of magnitudes of its charge and linear charge density $$\lambda$$ on the wire.

(iii) Draw a graph of kinetic energy as a function of linear charge density $$\lambda$$.

OR

(b) (i) Consider two identical point charges located at points $$(0,0)$$ and $$(a, 0)$$.

(1) Is there a point on the line joining them at which the electric field is zero?

(2) Is there a point on the line joining them at which the electric potential is zero?

Justify your answers for each case.

(ii) State the significance of negative value of electrostatic potential energy of a system of charges.

Three charges are placed at the corners of an equilateral triangle $$A B C$$ of side $$2.0 \mathrm{~m}$$ as shown in figure. Calculate the electric potential energy of the system of three charges.
questions.solution:
(a) (i) Electric field due to an infinitely long thin straight wire with uniform linear charge density:

Linear charge density $$=\lambda$$

A point $$\mathrm{P}$$ is considered at a distance $$x$$ where the electric field is to be determined.

A cylindrical Gaussian surface of length $$l$$ and radius $$x$$ is considered with the wire as its axis.

Magnitude of electric field at every point the curved surface of the cylinder is same and is directed outward making an angle $$0^0$$ with the direction of area vector.

Electric fields at the two flat surfaces of the cylinder are zero since, the direction of electric field through those surfaces and the direction of area vector are perpendicular.

So, total flux through the curved surface $$=\phi=E \times 2 \pi x l$$

According to Gauss' law, $$\phi=\frac{l \lambda}{\varepsilon_0}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \therefore & E \times 2 \pi x l & =\frac{l \lambda}{\varepsilon_0} \\ & \therefore & E & =\frac{1}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{\lambda}{x} \end{aligned}$$

(ii) Electric field produced by an infinitely long straight charged wire,

$$E=\frac{\lambda}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0 r}$$

This field provides the centripetal force to the revolving electron.

or, $$e E=\frac{m v^2}{r}$$

or, $$\frac{\lambda}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0 r}=\frac{m v^2}{r}$$

or, $$\quad m v^2=\frac{e \lambda}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0}$$

$$\therefore$$ Kinetic energy (KE) $$=\frac{1}{2} m v^2=\frac{e \lambda}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}$$

(iii) The required equation,

$$\frac{1}{2} m v^2=\frac{e \lambda}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}$$

It is the equation of a straight line passing through the origin.

OR

(b) (i) (1) Yes, Electric field will be zero at $$\left(\frac{a}{2}, 0\right)$$ point. At this point the magnitudes of both the electric fields are same in magnitude and are oppositely directed.

(2) Electric potential will not be zero at any point since at every point the potential will be the summation of both the potentials and both charges are of similar nature.

(ii) Significance of negative electrostatic potential energy :

Negative electrostatic potential energy of a system of charges means work is to be done against the field to move the charges apart.

Potential energy due to charges at A and B

$$U_1=\frac{k q_1 q_2}{r}$$

Or, $$U_1=\frac{9 \times 10^9 \times(-4) \times(4) \times 10^{-12}}{2}$$

$$\therefore \quad U_1=-72 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~J}$$

Potential energy due to charges at $$\mathrm{A}$$ and $$\mathrm{C}$$

$$U_2=\frac{k q_1 q_3}{r}$$

Or, $$\quad U_1=\frac{9 \times 10^9 \times(4) \times(2) \times 10^{-12}}{2}$$

$$\therefore \quad U_2=36 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~J}$$

Potential energy due to charges at B and C

$$U_3=\frac{k q_2 q_3}{r}$$

Or, $$U_3=\frac{9 \times 10^9 \times(-4) \times(2) \times 10^{-12}}{2}$$

$$\therefore \quad U_3=-36 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~J}$$

Net potential energy of the system of three charges.

$$\begin{aligned} & U =U_1+U_2+U_3 \\ \text { Or, } & U =-72 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~J}+36 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~J}-36 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~J} \\ \therefore & \quad U =-72 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~J} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Let $T_1$ and $T_2$ be two distinct common tangents to the ellipse $E: \frac{x^2}{6}+\frac{y^2}{3}=1$ and the parabola $P: y^2=12 x$. Suppose that the tangent $T_1$ touches $P$ and $E$ at the points $A_1$ and $A_2$, respectively and the tangent $T_2$ touches $P$ and $E$ at the points $A_4$ and $A_3$, respectively. Then which of the following statements is(are) true?
options: The area of the quadrilateral $A_1 A_2 A_3 A_4$ is 35 square units
The area of the quadrilateral $A_1 A_2 A_3 A_4$ is 36 square units
The tangents $T_1$ and $T_2$ meet the $x$-axis at the point $(-3,0)$
The tangents $T_1$ and $T_2$ meet the $x$-axis at the point $(-6,0)$
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & E: \frac{x^2}{6}+\frac{y^2}{3}=1 \text {, Tangent : } y=m_1 x \pm \sqrt{6 m_1^2+3} \\\\ & P: y^2=12 x, \text { Tangent: } y=m_2 x+\frac{3}{m_2} \end{aligned} $$

For common tangent

$$ \begin{aligned} & m=m_1=m_2, \pm \sqrt{6 m_1^2+3}=\frac{3}{m_2} \\\\ & \Rightarrow m= \pm 1 \end{aligned} $$

$\Rightarrow$ Equation of common tangents $y=x+3$ and $y=-x-3$ point of contact for parabola is $\left(\frac{a}{m^2}, \frac{2 a}{m}\right)$

$$ \Rightarrow A_1 \equiv(3,6), \quad A_4(3-6) $$

Let $A_2\left(x_1, y_1\right) \Rightarrow$ tangent to $E$ is $\frac{x x_1}{6}+\frac{y y_1}{3}=1$

$A_3$ is mirror image of $A_2$ in $x$-axis $\Rightarrow A_3(-2,-1)$


Intersection point of $T_1=0$ and $T_2=0$ is $(-3,0)$

Area of quadrilateral $A_1 A_2 A_3 A_4=\frac{1}{2}(12+2) \times 5=35$ square units

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: What is PE + PF equal to ?
questions.solution:
To determine the value of PE + PF, let's first recall the properties of an ellipse. An ellipse has the characteristic that the sum of the distances from any point on the ellipse to the foci is constant and is equal to the length of the major axis.

The equation of the given ellipse is:

$$\frac{x^2}{a^2} + \frac{y^2}{b^2} = 1$$

Here, we have:

$$x^2 + 4y^2 = 1$$

Comparing this with the standard form, we identify $ a $ and $ b $:

$$a^2 = 1$$

$$b^2 = \frac{1}{4}$$

Thus,

$$a = 1$$

$$b = \frac{1}{2}$$

The length of the major axis of an ellipse is given by $ 2a $. For this ellipse, the length of the major axis is:

$$2a = 2 \times 1 = 2$$

So, by the property of ellipses, the sum of the distances from any point on the ellipse to the foci (PE + PF) is equal to the length of the major axis. Therefore, PE + PF is:

$$2$$

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option B

2

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question:
Consider the following points :

1. $\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, 0\right)$

2. $\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, \frac{1}{4}\right)$

3. $\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2},-\frac{1}{4}\right)$

Which of the above points lie on latus rectum of ellipse ?
questions.solution:
To determine which of the given points lie on the latus rectum of the ellipse $ x^2 + 4y^2 = 1 $, we first need to understand the definition and properties of the latus rectum of an ellipse.

Properties of the Ellipse and Latus Rectum

The given ellipse is $ x^2 + 4y^2 = 1 $. This can be rewritten in standard form as:

$ \frac{x^2}{1} + \frac{y^2}{\frac{1}{4}} = 1 $

From this, we can see that:

  • The semi-major axis $ a = 1 $

  • The semi-minor axis $ b = \frac{1}{2} $

The foci of the ellipse are at:

$ (\pm c, 0) \text{ where } c = \sqrt{a^2 - b^2} = \sqrt{1 - \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2} = \sqrt{1 - \frac{1}{4}} = \sqrt{\frac{3}{4}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $

The latus rectum of an ellipse is a line segment perpendicular to the major axis and passing through a focus. The endpoints of the latus rectum are given by the x-coordinate of the foci and extend above and below the foci by $ 2b^2/a $.

Since $ a = 1 $ and $ b = \frac{1}{2} $:

$ 2b^2/a = 2 \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 / 1 = 2 \cdot \frac{1}{4} = \frac{1}{2} $

Thus, the latus rectum extends $\frac{1}{2}$ unit above and below the foci, which are located at $(\pm \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, 0)$.

Given Points and Latus Rectum

The points provided are:

  1. $\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, 0 \right)$

  2. $\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, \frac{1}{4} \right)$

  3. $\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, -\frac{1}{4} \right)$

These points have the x-coordinate $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$, which corresponds to the x-coordinate of one of the foci.

For a point to lie on the latus rectum, it must have the same x-coordinate as the foci and a y-coordinate within $\pm \frac{1}{2}$ of the focus.

Verification

  1. $\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, 0 \right)$ lies on the latus rectum since it is exactly at the focus.

  2. $\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, \frac{1}{4} \right)$ lies on the latus rectum since $\frac{1}{4} \leq \frac{1}{2}$.

  3. $\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, -\frac{1}{4} \right)$ lies on the latus rectum since $-\frac{1}{4} \geq -\frac{1}{2}$.

Therefore, all three points lie on the latus rectum.

The correct answer is Option D: 1, 2 and 3.

questions.title: BITSAT 2023
questions.question: Water is flowing on a horizontal fixed surface such that its flow velocity varies with $$y$$ (vertical direction) as $$v=k\left(\frac{2 y^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^3}{a^3}\right)$$. If coefficient of viscosity for water is $$2 \eta$$. What will the shear stress between layers of water at $$y=a$$ ?
options:
$$\frac{2 \eta k}{a}$$
$$\frac{\eta}{k a}$$
$$\frac{\eta a}{2 k}$$
None of these
questions.solution:
To determine the shear stress between layers of water at $$y=a$$, we start by using the given velocity profile and the definition of shear stress for a fluid in laminar flow.

The shear stress $$\tau$$ in a fluid is given by:

$$ \tau = \eta \frac{du}{dy} $$

where $$\eta$$ is the dynamic viscosity of the fluid, and $$\frac{du}{dy}$$ is the velocity gradient in the direction perpendicular to the flow.

Given the velocity profile:

$$ v = k \left( \frac{2 y^2}{a^2} - \frac{y^3}{a^3} \right) $$

First, we need to calculate the velocity gradient $$\frac{dv}{dy}$$:

$$ \frac{dv}{dy} = \frac{d}{dy} \left( k \left( \frac{2 y^2}{a^2} - \frac{y^3}{a^3} \right) \right) $$

Applying the differentiation, we get:

$$ \frac{dv}{dy} = k \left( \frac{d}{dy} \left( \frac{2 y^2}{a^2} \right) - \frac{d}{dy} \left( \frac{y^3}{a^3} \right) \right) $$

Differentiating term by term, we have:

$$ \frac{dv}{dy} = k \left( \frac{2 \cdot 2y}{a^2} - \frac{3y^2}{a^3} \right) = k \left( \frac{4y}{a^2} - \frac{3y^2}{a^3} \right) $$

We need to evaluate this gradient at $$y = a$$:

$$ \left. \frac{dv}{dy} \right|_{y=a} = k \left( \frac{4a}{a^2} - \frac{3a^2}{a^3} \right) = k \left( \frac{4a}{a^2} - \frac{3a^2}{a^3} \right) = k \left( \frac{4}{a} - \frac{3}{a} \right) = k \left( \frac{4-3}{a} \right) = \frac{k}{a} $$

Now, using the given viscosity of the water, $$2 \eta$$, the shear stress $$\tau$$ is given by:

$$ \tau = 2 \eta \left( \frac{k}{a} \right) = \frac{2 \eta k}{a} $$

Therefore, the shear stress between layers of water at $$y=a$$ is:

$$ \tau = \frac{2 \eta k}{a} $$

The correct option is Option A:

$$ \frac{2 \eta k}{a} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2023
questions.question: If $$f(x)=x^2-2 x+1$$ and $$f \circ g(x)=x^2+2 x+1$$, then $$g(x)$$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have, $$f(x)=x^2-2 x+1$$ and

$$\begin{array}{ll} & f \circ g(x)=x^2+2 x+1 \\ \therefore & f(g(x))=x^2+2 x+1 \\ \Rightarrow & (g(x))^2-2\left(g(x)+1=x^2+2 x+1\right. \\ \Rightarrow & (g(x))^2-2\left(g(x)-x^2-2 x=0\right. \\ \Rightarrow & g(x)=\frac{-(-2) \pm \sqrt{(-2)^2-4\left(-x^2-2 x\right)(1)}}{2(1)} \\ \Rightarrow & g(x)=\frac{2 \pm \sqrt{4+4 x^2+8 x}}{2} \\ \Rightarrow & g(x)=\frac{2 \pm 2 \sqrt{x^2+2 x+1}}{2} \\ \Rightarrow & g(x)=1 \pm(x+1) \\ \Rightarrow & g(x)=x+2-x \end{array}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Let $S=(0,1) \cup(1,2) \cup(3,4)$ and $T=\{0,1,2,3\}$. Then which of the following statements is(are) true?
options: There are infinitely many functions from $S$ to $T$
There are infinitely many strictly increasing functions from $S$ to $T$
The number of continuous functions from $S$ to $T$ is at most 120
Every continuous function from $S$ to $T$ is differentiable
questions.solution:
$$ S=(0,1) \cup(1,2) \cup(3,4) \text { and } T=\{0,1,2,3\} \text {. } $$

Let domain and co-domain of a function $y=f(x)$ are $S$ and $T$ respectively.

(A) There are infinitely many elements in domain and four elements in co-domain.

$\Rightarrow$ There are infinitely many functions from $S$ to $T$.

$\Rightarrow$ Option $(A)$ is correct

(B) If number of elements in domain is greater than number of elements in co-domain, then number of strictly increasing function is zero.

$\Rightarrow$ Option (B) is incorrect

(C) Maximum number of continuous functions = $4 \times 4 \times 4=64$

(Every subset $(0,1),(1,2),(3,4)$ has four choices)

$\because 64 < 120 \Rightarrow$ option (C) is correct.

(D) For every point at which $f(x)$ is continuous, $f(x)=0$

$\Rightarrow$ Every continuous function from $S$ to $T$ is differentiable.

Option (D) is correct.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Let $f:[0,1] \rightarrow[0,1]$ be the function defined by $f(x)=\frac{x^3}{3}-x^2+\frac{5}{9} x+\frac{17}{36}$. Consider the square region $S=[0,1] \times[0,1]$. Let $G=\{(x, y) \in S: y>f(x)\}$ be called the green region and $R=\{(x, y) \in S: y < f(x)\}$ be called the red region. Let $L_h=\{(x, h) \in S: x \in[0,1]\}$ be the horizontal line drawn at a height $h \in[0,1]$. Then which of the following statements is(are) true?
options: There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the green region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the green region below the line $L_h$
There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the red region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the red region below the line $L_h$
There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the green region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the red region below the line $L_h$
There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the red region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the green region below the line $L_k$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \text { Given } f:[0,1] & \rightarrow[0,1] \\\\ f(x) & =\frac{x^3}{3}-x^2+\frac{5}{9} x+\frac{17}{36} \\\\ f^{\prime}(x) & =\frac{3 x^2}{3}-2 x+\frac{5}{9}\end{aligned}$

$\begin{aligned} & \quad f^{\prime}(x)=0 \\\\ & 9 x^2-18 x+5=0 \\\\ & \Rightarrow 9 x^2-15 x-3 x+5=0 \\\\ & \Rightarrow 3 x(3 x-5)-1(3 x-5)=0 \\\\ & \Rightarrow(3 x-5)(3 x-1)=0 \\\\ & \Rightarrow x=\frac{1}{3} \text { or } \frac{5}{3} \\\\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=2 x-2 \\\\ & f^{\prime \prime}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)=\frac{2}{3}-2<0 \text { point of maxima }\end{aligned}$


$\begin{aligned} \text { Area }_{\text {red }} & =\int_0^1 f(x) d x \\\\ & =\left[\frac{x^4}{12}-\frac{x^3}{3}+\frac{5 x^2}{18}+\frac{17 x}{36}\right]_0^1 \\\\ & =\frac{1}{12}-\frac{1}{3}+\frac{5}{18}+\frac{17}{36} \\\\ & =\frac{3-12+10+17}{36} \\\\ & =\frac{18}{36}=\frac{1}{2}=0.5 \\\\ \therefore (\text { Area })_{\text {green }} & =1-\frac{1}{2}=0.5\end{aligned}$

(A) $1-h=h-\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow h=\frac{3}{4}, \frac{3}{4}>\frac{2}{3}$ option (A) is incorrect

(B) $h=\frac{1}{2}-h \Rightarrow h=\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow$ option (B) is correct.

(C) $\int\limits_0^1 f(x) d x=\frac{1}{2}, \int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{2} d x=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \int\limits_0^1\left(f(x)-\frac{1}{2}\right) d x=0$

$\Rightarrow h=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow$ option (C) is correct.

(D) $\because$ Option (C) is correct $\Rightarrow$ option (D) is also correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: At what value of x does the function attain minimum value ?
questions.solution:
To determine the value of $x$ for which the function $f(x)$ attains its minimum value, we need to consider the expression given:

$$f(x) = |x - 2| + |3 - x| + |4 - x|$$

To find the minimum value, we need to analyze the behavior of the function at different intervals defined by the critical points (2, 3, and 4). Let's examine each interval:

1. $x \leq 2$

For $x \leq 2$, all terms are non-negative. Therefore:

$$f(x) = (2 - x) + (3 - x) + (4 - x) = 9 - 3x$$

2. $2 < x \leq 3$

For $2 < x \leq 3$, the first term is non-positive and the other terms are non-negative. Therefore:

$$f(x) = (x - 2) + (3 - x) + (4 - x) = x - 2 - x + 3 + 4 - x = 5 - x$$

3. $3 < x \leq 4$

For $3 < x \leq 4$, the first two terms are non-positive and the last term is non-negative. Therefore:

$$f(x) = (x - 2) + (x - 3) + (4 - x) = x - 2 + x - 3 + 4 - x = x - 1$$

4. $x > 4$

For $x > 4$, all terms are non-positive. Therefore:

$$f(x) = (x - 2) + (x - 3) + (x - 4) = 3x - 9$$

Next, we need to identify the minimum value from each of the intervals:

  • For $x \leq 2$, $f(x) = 9 - 3x$, which is decreasing as $x$ increases.
  • For $2 < x \leq 3$, $f(x) = 5 - x$, which is decreasing until $x = 3$.
  • For $3 < x \leq 4$, $f(x) = x - 1$, which is increasing as $x$ increases.
  • For $x > 4$, $f(x) = 3x - 9$, which is increasing as $x$ increases.

At $x = 3$, between two intervals, the value of $f(x)$ can be calculated as follows:

$$f(3) = |3 - 2| + |3 - 3| + |4 - 3| = 1 + 0 + 1 = 2$$

Thus, the function attains its minimum value of 2 at $x = 3$.

The correct answer is:

Option B: 3

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: What is the minimum value of the function ?
questions.solution:
To find the minimum value of the function $ f(x) = |x - 2| + |3 - x| + |4 - x| $, we need to consider the behavior of the absolute value function. The absolute value function $ |a| $ is defined as $ a $ if $ a \geq 0 $ and $ -a $ if $ a < 0 $.

Analyzing the Function:

The function involves the sum of three absolute value terms. The critical points where the expressions inside the absolute values change sign are $ x = 2 $, $ x = 3 $, and $ x = 4 $. Therefore, we need to analyze the function in the intervals created by these points: $ (-\infty, 2) $, $ (2, 3) $, $ (3, 4) $, and $ (4, \infty) $.

Case 1: $ x \leq 2 $

When $ x \leq 2 $:

$ |x - 2| = 2 - x $

$ |3 - x| = 3 - x $

$ |4 - x| = 4 - x $

Thus,

$ f(x) = (2 - x) + (3 - x) + (4 - x) = 9 - 3x $

Case 2: $ 2 < x \leq 3 $

When $ 2 < x \leq 3 $:

$ |x - 2| = x - 2 $

$ |3 - x| = 3 - x $

$ |4 - x| = 4 - x $

Thus,

$ f(x) = (x - 2) + (3 - x) + (4 - x) = 1 + 4 - x = 5 - x $

Case 3: $ 3 < x \leq 4 $

When $ 3 < x \leq 4 $:

$ |x - 2| = x - 2 $

$ |3 - x| = x - 3 $

$ |4 - x| = 4 - x $

Thus,

$ f(x) = (x - 2) + (x - 3) + (4 - x) = x - 2 + x - 3 + 4 - x = x - 1 $

Case 4: $ x > 4 $

When $ x > 4 $:

$ |x - 2| = x - 2 $

$ |3 - x| = x - 3 $

$ |4 - x| = x - 4 $

Thus,

$ f(x) = (x - 2) + (x - 3) + (x - 4) = 3x - 9 $

Finding the Minimum Value:

Now, we determine the minimum value in each interval:

  1. For $ x \leq 2 $:

$ f(x) = 9 - 3x $

The minimum value in this interval occurs at $ x = 2 $:

$ f(2) = 9 - 3(2) = 9 - 6 = 3 $

  1. For $ 2 < x \leq 3 $:

$ f(x) = 5 - x $

The minimum value in this interval occurs at $ x = 3 $:

$ f(3) = 5 - 3 = 2 $

  1. For $ 3 < x \leq 4 $:

$ f(x) = x - 1 $

The minimum value in this interval occurs at $ x = 3 $:

$ f(3) = 3 - 1 = 2 $

  1. For $ x > 4 $:

$ f(x) = 3x - 9 $

This function increases as $ x $ increases, so it does not provide a minimum value within this interval.

Comparing the minimum values in each interval, the overall minimum value of $ f(x) $ is:

$ \boxed{2} $

Therefore, the minimum value of the function is:

Option A: 2.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: What is f(0) equal to ?
questions.solution:
To find $ f(0) $, we need to evaluate the determinant of the given matrix at $ x = 0 $. The matrix is:

$$ \left|\begin{array}{ccc} \cos x & x & 1 \\ 2 \sin x & x^2 & 2x \\ \tan x & x & 1 \end{array}\right| $$

Substituting $ x = 0 $ into the matrix, we get:

$$ \left|\begin{array}{ccc} \cos 0 & 0 & 1 \\ 2 \sin 0 & 0^2 & 2 \cdot 0 \\ \tan 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right| $$

Since $ \cos 0 = 1 $, $ \sin 0 = 0 $, and $ \tan 0 = 0 $, the matrix simplifies to:

$$ \left|\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right| $$

Now, the determinant of this matrix is calculated as follows:

$$ \left|\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right| = 1 \left( \left| \begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right| \right) - 0 \left( \left| \begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right| \right) + 1 \left( \left| \begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right| \right) $$

Calculating the smaller determinants inside the expression, we have:

$$ \left| \begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right| = (0 \cdot 1) - (0 \cdot 0) = 0 $$

$$ \left| \begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right| = 0 $$

$$ \left| \begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right| = (0 \cdot 0) - (0 \cdot 0) = 0 $$

So, putting it all together we have:

$$ f(0) = 1 \cdot 0 - 0 \cdot 0 + 1 \cdot 0 = 0 $$

Therefore, the value of $ f(0) $ is 0. The correct answer is:

Option B: 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: What is $f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ equal to ?
questions.solution:
To find $ f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) $, we first need to understand the function given:

$$ f(x) = \sin[\pi^2]x + \cos[-\pi^2]x $$

Here, the brackets $[.]$ represent the greatest integer function (also known as the floor function), which takes any real number and rounds it down to the nearest integer.

Let's break down the function step-by-step:

1. Calculate $\pi^2$:

$$\pi^2 \approx 3.14159^2 \approx 9.8696$$

2. Apply the greatest integer function to $\pi^2$ and $-\pi^2$:

$$[\pi^2] = 9$$

$$[-\pi^2] = -10$$

3. Substitute these into the sine and cosine functions:

$$ f(x) = \sin(9x) + \cos(-10x) $$

Now, let's evaluate the function at $ x = \frac{\pi}{2} $:

$$ f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) = \sin(9 \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}) + \cos(-10 \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}) $$

Simplify the terms inside the trigonometric functions:

$$ 9 \cdot \frac{\pi}{2} = \frac{9\pi}{2} $$

$$ -10 \cdot \frac{\pi}{2} = -5\pi $$

Now, recall the periodicity of the sine and cosine functions:

$$ \sin\left(\frac{9\pi}{2}\right) = \sin\left(4\pi + \frac{\pi}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) = 1 $$

$$ \cos(-5\pi) = \cos(5\pi) = \cos(\pi) = -1 $$

Combining these results, we get:

$$ f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) = 1 + (-1) = 0 $$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: What is $f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$ equal to ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $$f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$$, we need to evaluate the given function:

$$f(x) = \sin\left[\pi^2\right]x + \cos\left[-\pi^2\right]x$$

First, recall that the greatest integer function $$[.]$$ represents the floor function, which returns the greatest integer less than or equal to the given number. Now, let's evaluate the arguments inside the trigonometric functions.

Since $$\pi^2$$ is approximately 9.869, we have:

  • $$\left[\pi^2\right] = [9.869] = 9$$
  • $$\left[-\pi^2\right] = [-9.869] = -10$$ (because $$\left[-x\right]$$ finds the nearest integer less than or equal to $$-x$$)

Therefore, the function simplifies to:

$$f(x) = \sin(9x) + \cos(-10x)$$

Using the fact that $$\cos(-\theta) = \cos(\theta)$$, we can write:

$$f(x) = \sin(9x) + \cos(10x)$$

Now, we substitute $$x = \frac{\pi}{4}$$:

$$f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \sin\left( 9 \cdot \frac{\pi}{4} \right) + \cos\left( 10 \cdot \frac{\pi}{4} \right)$$

This simplifies further to:

$$f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{9\pi}{4}\right) + \cos\left(\frac{5\pi}{2}\right)$$

Next, recall that the sine and cosine functions are periodic with $$2\pi$$. Therefore:

  • $$\sin\left(\frac{9\pi}{4}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{9\pi}{4} - 2\pi\right) = \sin\left(\frac{9\pi}{4} - \frac{8\pi}{4}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$$
  • $$\cos\left(\frac{5\pi}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{5\pi}{2} - 2\pi\right) = \cos\left(\frac{5\pi}{2} - \frac{4\pi}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) = 0$$

So:

$$f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} + 0 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$$

Therefore, the correct answer is Option D:

$$ \frac{1}{\sqrt 2}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: If f(x) = x<sup>2</sup> + 2 and g(x) = 2x - 3, then what is (fog)(1) equal to ?
questions.solution:
To find $(f \circ g)(1)$, we need to evaluate the function $f$ at the point $g(1)$. Let's break this down step by step.

First, we need to find the value of $g(1)$:

Given $g(x) = 2x - 3$, we can plug in $x = 1$:

$$ g(1) = 2(1) - 3 = 2 - 3 = -1 $$

Now that we have $g(1) = -1$, we need to evaluate $f(-1)$:

Given $f(x) = x^2 + 2$, we can plug in $x = -1$:

$$ f(-1) = (-1)^2 + 2 = 1 + 2 = 3 $$

Therefore, $(f \circ g)(1) = f(g(1)) = f(-1) = 3$.

So, the correct option is A:

3

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 16 April 2023
questions.question: What is the range of the function f(x) = x + |x| if the domain is the set of real numbers ?
questions.solution:
To determine the range of the function $ f(x) = x + |x| $ where the domain is the set of real numbers, we first need to understand how the function behaves for different values of $ x $.

Recall that the absolute value function $ |x| $ is defined as follows:

$$ |x| = \begin{cases} -x & \text{if } x < 0 \\ x & \text{if } x \geq 0 \end{cases} $$

Now, let’s consider the two cases for $ x $:

Case 1: $ x \geq 0 $

If $ x \geq 0 $, then $ |x| = x $. So the function $ f(x) $ becomes:

$$ f(x) = x + |x| = x + x = 2x $$

Since $ x \geq 0 $, the expression $ 2x \geq 0 $. Therefore, for $ x \geq 0 $, the function $ f(x) $ can take on any value from 0 to $ \infty $.

Case 2: $ x < 0 $

If $ x < 0 $, then $ |x| = -x $. So the function $ f(x) $ becomes:

$$ f(x) = x + |x| = x - x = 0 $$

Hence, for $ x < 0 $, the function $ f(x) $ always equals 0.

Combining these two cases, we see that the function $ f(x) = x + |x| $ can take on any value in the interval $[0, \infty)$. The value 0 is achieved when $ x \leq 0 $, and any positive value is achieved for some $ x \geq 0 $.

Therefore, the range of the function $ f(x) = x + |x| $ is $[0, \infty)$.

The correct option is:

Option B: [0, ∞)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 3 September 2023
questions.question: A function satisfies $f(x-y)=\frac{f(x)}{f(y)}$, where f(y) ≠ 0. If f(1) = 0.5, then what is f(2) + f(3) + f(4) + f(5) + f(6) equal to ?
questions.solution:
We are given:

$ f(x-y) = \frac{f(x)}{f(y)}, \quad f(y) \neq 0, \quad f(1) = \tfrac{1}{2} $

We are asked to find:

$ f(2) + f(3) + f(4) + f(5) + f(6) $

Step 1. Put $x = y$.

$ f(x-x) = f(0) = \frac{f(x)}{f(x)} = 1 $

So,

$ f(0) = 1 $

Step 2. Put $y = 0$.

$ f(x-0) = f(x) = \frac{f(x)}{f(0)} = \frac{f(x)}{1} $

This is consistent.

Step 3. Try $x=2, y=1$.

$ f(2-1) = f(1) = \frac{f(2)}{f(1)} $

$ f(1) = \frac{f(2)}{f(1)} \implies f(2) = f(1)^2 $

Since $f(1) = \tfrac{1}{2}$,

$ f(2) = \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^2 = \tfrac{1}{4} $

Step 4. Similarly, put $x=3, y=1$.

$ f(3-1) = f(2) = \frac{f(3)}{f(1)} $

$ f(3) = f(2) f(1) = \tfrac{1}{4}\cdot\tfrac{1}{2} = \tfrac{1}{8} $

Step 5. Next, for $x=4, y=1$.

$ f(4-1) = f(3) = \frac{f(4)}{f(1)} $

$ f(4) = f(3) f(1) = \tfrac{1}{8}\cdot\tfrac{1}{2} = \tfrac{1}{16} $

Step 6. For $x=5, y=1$:

$ f(5-1) = f(4) = \frac{f(5)}{f(1)} $

$ f(5) = f(4) f(1) = \tfrac{1}{16}\cdot\tfrac{1}{2} = \tfrac{1}{32} $

Step 7. For $x=6, y=1$:

$ f(6-1) = f(5) = \frac{f(6)}{f(1)} $

$ f(6) = f(5) f(1) = \tfrac{1}{32}\cdot\tfrac{1}{2} = \tfrac{1}{64} $

Step 8. Add all required values.

$ f(2) + f(3) + f(4) + f(5) + f(6) = \tfrac{1}{4} + \tfrac{1}{8} + \tfrac{1}{16} + \tfrac{1}{32} + \tfrac{1}{64} $

Take LCM as 64:

$ = \frac{16}{64} + \frac{8}{64} + \frac{4}{64} + \frac{2}{64} + \frac{1}{64} $

$ = \frac{31}{64} $

Final Answer:

$ \boxed{\tfrac{31}{64}} $

So, the correct option is D.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following is a possible expression for g(x)?
questions.solution:
To determine a possible expression for $ g(x) $, start by considering the compositions of $ g $ and $ f $.

Given $ f(x) = x^2 - 1 $ and $ (g \circ f)(x) = x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $:

$$ g(f(x)) = g(x^2 - 1) = x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $$

We want to find $ g(x) $ such that $ g(x^2 - 1) = x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $. Let's check each option:

Option A: $ \sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt[4]{x+1} $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} - \sqrt[4]{(x^2-1)+1} = \sqrt{x^2} - \sqrt[4]{x^2} = x - x^{1/2} $$

This does not match $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

Option B: $ \sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt[4]{x+1}+1 $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} - \sqrt[4]{(x^2-1)+1} + 1 = \sqrt{x^2} - \sqrt[4]{x^2} + 1 = x - x^{1/2} + 1 $$

This matches $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

Option C: $ \sqrt{x+1}+\sqrt[4]{x+1} $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} + \sqrt[4]{(x^2-1)+1} = \sqrt{x^2} + \sqrt[4]{x^2} = x + x^{1/2} $$

This does not match $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

Option D: $ x+1-\sqrt{x+1}+1 $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = (x^2 - 1) + 1 - \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} + 1 = x^2 - \sqrt{x^2} + 1 = x^2 - x + 1 $$

This does not match $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

The possible expression for $ g(x) $ is:

$$ \sqrt{x+1} - \sqrt[4]{x+1} + 1 $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 3 September 2023
questions.question: What is g(15) equal to ?
questions.solution:
To solve for $ g(15) $, we need to use the expression $ g(x) = \sqrt{x + 1} - \sqrt[4]{x + 1} + 1 $.

Let's substitute $ x $ with $ 15 $:

$ g(15) = \sqrt{15 + 1} - \sqrt[4]{15 + 1} + 1 $

Next, simplify each term:

$ g(15) = \sqrt{16} - \sqrt[4]{16} + 1 $

Since $ \sqrt{16} = 4 $ and $ \sqrt[4]{16} = 2 $, we have:

$ g(15) = 4 - 2 + 1 $

Therefore:

$ g(15) = 3 $

Hence, $ g(15) $ equals 3.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 3 September 2023
questions.question: What is the range of the function?
questions.solution:
The function is defined by:

$$ f(x) = \pi + \sin^2(x) $$

Given that $ \sin^2(x) $ represents the square of the sine function, the values of $ \sin^2(x) $ lie between 0 and 1, inclusive, because squaring the sine function (which oscillates between -1 and 1) yields values from 0 to 1. Therefore, the minimum value of $ \sin^2(x) $ is 0 and the maximum value is 1.

Adding $ \pi $ to $ \sin^2(x) $:

When $ \sin^2(x) = 0 $:

$$ f(x) = \pi + 0 = \pi $$

When $ \sin^2(x) = 1 $:

$$ f(x) = \pi + 1 $$

Thus, the range of the function $ f(x) $ is from $ \pi $ to $ \pi + 1 $, inclusive.

The correct option is:

$ [\pi, \pi + 1] $

Option B

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 3 September 2023
questions.question: What is the domain of the function?
questions.solution:
The domain of the function

$$ f(x) = \sqrt{2 - x} + \sqrt{2 + x} $$

is determined by ensuring that the expressions under the square roots are non-negative. Thus:

$$ 2 - x \geq 0 \quad \text{and} \quad 2 + x \geq 0 $$

Solving these inequalities, we get:

$$ x \leq 2 \quad \text{and} \quad x \geq -2 $$

Therefore, the domain of $ f(x) $ is:

$$ [-2, 2] $$

So, the correct option is Option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 3 September 2023
questions.question: What is the greatest value of the function?
questions.solution:
$ f(x)=\sqrt{2-x}+\sqrt{2+x} $

Let $ f(x) = y $.

Squaring both sides, we get:

$ [f(x)]^2 = y^2 = (2-x) + (2+x) + 2\sqrt{(2-x)(2+x)} $

Simplifying the equation:

$ y^2 = 4 + 2\sqrt{4-x^2} $

The value of $ y^2 $ is maximized when $ x $ is at its minimum value, which is 0.

Hence, the function reaches its maximum when $ x = 0 $.

The greatest value is given by:

$ y^2_{\text{max}} = 4 + 2\sqrt{4-0^2} $

$ y^2_{\text{max}} = 8 $

Therefore:

$ y_{\text{max}} = \sqrt{8} $

The greatest value of the function $ \sqrt{2-x} + \sqrt{2+x} $ is $ \sqrt{8} $.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
A gas has a compressibility factor of 0.5 and a molar volume of $0.4 ~\mathrm{dm}^3 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ at a temperature of $800 \mathrm{~K}$ and pressure $\mathbf{x}$ atm. If it shows ideal gas behaviour at the same temperature and pressure, the molar volume will be $\mathbf{y} ~\mathrm{dm}^3 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. The value of $\mathbf{x} / \mathbf{y}$ is __________.

[Use: Gas constant, $\mathrm{R}=8 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~L}$ atm $\mathrm{K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ]
questions.solution:
For gas : $\mathrm{Z}=0.5, \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{m}}=0.4 \mathrm{~L} / \mathrm{mol}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T}=800 \mathrm{~K}, \mathrm{P}=\mathrm{X} \text { atm. } \\\\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{Z}=\frac{\mathrm{PV}_{\mathrm{m}}}{\mathrm{RT}} \\\\ \Rightarrow & \frac{\mathrm{X}(0.4)}{0.08 \times 800}=0.5 \\\\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{X}=80 \end{aligned} $$

For ideal gas, $\mathrm{PV}_{\mathrm{m}}=\mathrm{RT}$

$$ \Rightarrow \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{m}}=\frac{\mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{P}}=\frac{0.08 \times 800}{80}=0.8 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}=\mathrm{y} $$

Then, $\frac{x}{y}=\frac{80}{0.8}=100$.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question: The UN COP-27 Summit relates to:
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Climate change.

Here's why:

The UN COP-27 Summit stands for the 27th Conference of the Parties of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change. It's an annual event where world leaders, scientists, activists, and other stakeholders gather to discuss and negotiate global action on climate change. The primary focus of these conferences is:

  • Assessing the global climate situation: The summit reviews the current state of the climate, analyzes data, and evaluates the progress made in combating climate change.
  • Setting targets and commitments: Countries commit to specific goals and actions to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and adapt to the impacts of climate change.
  • Developing solutions: COP summits facilitate the development of new technologies, policies, and financial mechanisms to address climate change.

The other options are unrelated to the COP-27 Summit:

  • Option A: Russia-Ukraine war: This is a geopolitical conflict with no direct connection to the COP-27 Summit.
  • Option B: Terrorism and counter-terrorism: While climate change can contribute to instability and conflict, the COP-27 Summit is not specifically focused on terrorism.
  • Option D: Interpol: Interpol is an international law enforcement agency and is not involved in the COP-27 Summit.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: Area wise Madhya Pradesh is the fourth largest state in India
Hornbill festival is celebrated in Nagaland
Gujarat state contribute 7.9 per cent share to the National GDP
Telangana is situated in the Deccan plateau in the central stretch of Indian peninsula
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement to identify the one that is NOT correct:

Option A: "Area wise Madhya Pradesh is the fourth largest state in India."

Madhya Pradesh is actually the second-largest state in India in terms of area, after Rajasthan. Therefore, this statement is incorrect.

Option B: "Hornbill festival is celebrated in Nagaland."

The Hornbill Festival is indeed celebrated in Nagaland. It is one of the most popular festivals in the state, known for its vibrant cultural displays.

Option C: "Gujarat state contributes 7.9 per cent share to the National GDP."

Gujarat's contribution to India's GDP is quite significant, and while the exact percentage may vary slightly based on recent economic data, a contribution of around 7.9% is a reasonable approximation. Therefore, this statement is generally accepted as correct.

Option D: "Telangana is situated in the Deccan plateau in the central stretch of Indian peninsula."

Telangana is indeed located on the Deccan Plateau, which is part of the central stretch of the Indian peninsula. Hence, this statement is correct.

Based on this analysis, the statement that is NOT correct is:

Option A: Area wise Madhya Pradesh is the fourth largest state in India.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Who among the following is known as the architect of Indian planning?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C, P.C. Mahalanobis.

P.C. Mahalanobis is widely recognized as the architect of Indian planning due to his pivotal role in shaping the country's economic policies after independence. He is best known for introducing the Mahalanobis model of economic development, which heavily emphasized the development of heavy industries and a focus on self-reliance.

Here's why the other options are incorrect:

Option A, V.K.R.V. Rao: While V.K.R.V. Rao was a renowned economist and played a significant role in Indian economic planning, he was more focused on agricultural development and social welfare aspects. He is considered a champion of rural development and was instrumental in shaping India's Five-Year Plans.

Option B, Sukhamoy Chakravarty: Sukhamoy Chakravarty was a prominent economist who later served as the chairman of the Planning Commission. However, he primarily contributed to the theoretical and mathematical aspects of economic planning, rather than being the architect of the overall framework.

Option D, John Mathai: John Mathai was the first Finance Minister of independent India. While he played a role in the initial stages of economic planning, his contributions were more focused on financial management and policy implementation.

In conclusion, P.C. Mahalanobis's significant contributions, particularly the Mahalanobis model, established him as the architect of Indian planning, guiding the nation's economic development strategy for several decades after independence.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question:
Which among the following statements regarding the election of non-permanent members to the United Nations Security Council is/are correct?

1. Five members coming from Africa and Asia, one from Eastern Europe, two from Latin America and two from Western Europe and other states

2. Six members are elected each year by the General Assembly for two year term

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding the election of non-permanent members to the United Nations Security Council:

Statement 1: "Five members coming from Africa and Asia, one from Eastern Europe, two from Latin America and two from Western Europe and other states"

This statement is correct. The allocation of the ten non-permanent seats on the United Nations Security Council is distributed among various regional groups as follows:

  • 5 seats for African and Asian States
  • 1 seat for Eastern European States
  • 2 seats for Latin American and Caribbean States
  • 2 seats for Western European and other States

Statement 2: "Six members are elected each year by the General Assembly for a two-year term"

This statement is incorrect. The General Assembly elects five non-permanent members each year for a two-year term. This ensures a rotational system where five new members join the Security Council each year, resulting in overlapping terms.

Given our analysis of each statement, the correct answer is:

Option A: 1 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about Centralized Laboratory Net- work (CLN):

1. CLN works to test vaccines can be used during which pandemic

2. India is a member of CLN

3. CLN is a part of the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI)

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: 1, 2 and 3. Here's why:

1. CLN works to test vaccines can be used during which pandemic

This statement is correct. The Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN) is a global network of laboratories established to accelerate the development and testing of vaccines during a pandemic. Its main purpose is to provide a centralized platform for coordinating and standardizing vaccine testing procedures, ensuring quicker and more efficient vaccine development.

2. India is a member of CLN

This statement is also correct. India is an active member of the Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN). The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) is a key participant in the CLN, contributing its expertise and resources to the network's activities.

3. CLN is a part of the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI)

This statement is correct. The Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN) is indeed a part of the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI). CEPI is a global partnership that works to accelerate the development of vaccines against emerging infectious diseases. CLN serves as a critical component within CEPI's overall strategy for pandemic preparedness.

In summary, all three statements accurately describe the Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN) and its role in global pandemic preparedness.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about Kavach, the automatic train protection system:

1. It is designed and developed by Research Design and Standard Organisation (RDSO) in collaboration with Railway Technical Research Institute (RTRI), Japan

2. The trials of Kavach were facilitated by the Southern Railway

3. Kavach is a state-of-the-art electronic system with Safety Integrity Level-4 (SIL-4)

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each of the statements about Kavach, the automatic train protection system:

1. It is designed and developed by Research Design and Standard Organisation (RDSO) in collaboration with Railway Technical Research Institute (RTRI), Japan

This statement is incorrect. The Kavach system was designed and developed indigenously by the Research Design and Standard Organisation (RDSO) in India in collaboration with multiple local firms and not specifically with the Railway Technical Research Institute (RTRI) of Japan.

2. The trials of Kavach were facilitated by the Southern Railway

This statement is also incorrect. The trials of Kavach were facilitated primarily by the South Central Railway (SCR) zone, not the Southern Railway.

3. Kavach is a state-of-the-art electronic system with Safety Integrity Level-4 (SIL-4)

This statement is correct. Kavach is indeed a state-of-the-art electronic system that has Safety Integrity Level-4 (SIL-4), which is the highest level of safety integrity.

Therefore, only statement 3 is correct.

The correct option is:

Option B: 3 only

questions.title: BITSAT 2023
questions.question: In electrophilic aromatic substitution reaction, the nitro group is meta-directing because it
options:
decreases electron density at ortho-and para-positions
decreases electron density at meta-position
increases electron density at meta-position
increases electron density at ortho-and para-positions
questions.solution:
In electrophilic aromatic substitution (EAS) reactions, the nitro group ($$ \text{NO}_2 $$) is known to be a strong electron-withdrawing group due to its resonance and inductive effects. This property significantly influences the electron distribution in the aromatic ring, thereby affecting the position where incoming electrophiles are most likely to attack.

Let's analyze the options provided in this context:

  • Option A: Decreases electron density at ortho-and para-positions
  • Option B: Decreases electron density at meta-position
  • Option C: Increases electron density at meta-position
  • Option D: Increases electron density at ortho-and para-positions

The nitro group, being electron-withdrawing, pulls electron density away from the aromatic ring through two main mechanisms:

  • Inductive Effect: The nitro group withdraws electron density through the sigma bonds due to its high electronegativity.
  • Resonance Effect: The nitro group can participate in resonance, pulling electron density away from the ring, particularly affecting the ortho and para positions relative to the nitro group.

Due to the electron-withdrawing nature of the nitro group, electron density is significantly reduced at the ortho and para positions more than the meta positions. This makes the ortho and para positions less reactive towards electrophiles since electrophiles are attracted to electron-rich areas.

As a result, the meta position remains relatively electron-rich in comparison to the ortho and para positions. Thus, the electrophilic substitution is more likely to occur at the meta position.

Based on this understanding, the correct answer is:

Option A: Decreases electron density at ortho-and para-positions

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. Hypocenter is the point on the surface of the Earth, nearest to the focus.

2. Velocity of earthquake waves is higher in denser materials.

3. P waves move faster and are the first to arrive at the surface of the Earth.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Step 1: Recall the basic definitions.

  • Focus (Hypocenter): The point inside the Earth where an earthquake actually originates.

  • Epicenter: The point on the surface of the Earth vertically above the focus.

So, statement 1 is wrong because it confuses hypocenter (inside the Earth) with epicenter (on the surface).

Step 2: Velocity of earthquake waves.

  • Seismic waves travel faster in denser and more rigid materials.

So, statement 2 is correct.

Step 3: Types of seismic body waves.

  • P waves (Primary waves) are longitudinal waves.

  • They are the fastest seismic waves and hence reach the surface first.

So, statement 3 is correct.

Final Answer:

Correct statements are 2 and 3.

Answer: Option B (2 and 3)

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a cold current?

  3. Western Australian Current

questions.solution:
1. Podsolization is a soil-forming process characterized by intense leaching of iron and aluminium oxides from the upper horizons and their deposition in lower horizons.

  2. This process requires:

    • Cold to cool temperatures

    • High rainfall or moisture

    • Acidic litter (especially from coniferous trees)

  3. Among the given forest types:

    • Equatorial forest – hot and very wet; deep weathering but not podsolization

    • Monsoon forest – seasonal rainfall; less acidic soils

    • Taiga forest – cold climate, coniferous cover, acidic litter, high moisture

    • Mediterranean forest – hot dry summers, not ideal for podsolization

  4. Therefore, podsolization is predominantly found in Taiga forests.

Answer: Option C Taiga forest.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. Distance between the longitudes becomes zero on North Pole and South Pole.

2. Distance between the longitudes is maximum on the Equator.

3. Number of longitudes is more than number of latitudes.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement:

Distance between the longitudes becomes zero on North Pole and South Pole.

This statement is correct because all meridians (lines of longitude) converge at the poles. Therefore, the distance between any two longitudes at the poles is zero.

Distance between the longitudes is maximum on the Equator.

This statement is also correct. The distance between lines of longitude is greatest at the equator because it is the widest part of the Earth.

Number of longitudes is more than number of latitudes.

This statement is incorrect. There are 360 degrees of longitude (measured from 0° to 360°), but there are only 181 lines of latitude (from 90°N to 90°S including the equator).

Based on the analysis, the correct statements are 1 and 2. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option D 1 and 2 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question: According to Koppen's climatic classifications, the Great Northern Plains of India have which one of the following climates?
questions.solution:
According to Köppen's climatic classifications, the Great Northern Plains of India have the following climate:

Option B: Cwg climate

The Cwg climate refers to a monsoon-influenced humid subtropical climate, with dry winters and hot summers, which is characteristic of the Great Northern Plains of India.

Here's a brief description of the relevant climate types for better understanding:

Aw climate: Tropical savanna climate with dry winters.

Cwg climate: Monsoon-influenced humid subtropical climate with dry winters.

Amw climate: Monsoon climate with dry winters.

Dfc climate: Snow climate with cold winters and no dry season.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question: In the soil-forming regime, which one of the following occurs in a region where evapotranspiration exceeds precipitation significantly?
questions.solution:
In a region where evapotranspiration significantly exceeds precipitation, the soil-forming process most commonly observed is calcification.

Answer: Option A: Calcification

Explanation:

Calcification typically occurs in environments with low rainfall and high rates of evaporation, leading to the accumulation of calcium carbonate in the soil.

Laterization is associated with humid tropical and subtropical regions with high rainfall, leading to the leaching of silica and the concentration of iron and aluminum oxides.

Podsolization occurs in cool, moist climates, often under coniferous forests, where leaching leads to the accumulation of organic material and aluminum oxides.

Gleization is found in poorly drained, waterlogged areas where the soil remains saturated, leading to anaerobic conditions and the accumulation of organic matter.

Thus, the correct choice is Calcification (Option A).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following is found in the innermost part of the Earth?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: Lehmann discontinuity.

Here is a brief explanation of each discontinuity:

Conrad Discontinuity

Marks the boundary between the upper continental crust and the lower continental crust.

Moho Discontinuity (Mohorovičić discontinuity)

It is the boundary between the Earth's crust and the mantle.

Gutenberg Discontinuity

It separates the Earth's mantle from the outer core.

Lehmann Discontinuity

It lies between the outer core and the inner core, making it the innermost discontinuity in the Earth.

Therefore, the Lehmann discontinuity is found in the innermost part of the Earth.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following is the lowermost/innermost intrusive igneous rock?
questions.solution:
The lowermost/innermost intrusive igneous rock among the options provided is a Batholith.

A Batholith is a large, deep-seated, intrusive igneous rock body that has solidified from magma deep within the Earth's crust. It's typically the deepest of the intrusive formations listed.

Here is a brief explanation of each term:

Laccolith: A dome-shaped intrusive body that has caused the overlaying rocks to arch upwards.

Batholith: A massive and deep-seated intrusive body, typically larger than 100 square kilometers.

Lopolith: A large, concave (bowl-shaped) intrusive body.

Phacolith: A lens-shaped intrusion typically found in fold regions, like anticlines or synclines.

Therefore, the correct option is:

B. Batholith

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question: If it is 12 noon in New Delhi, what will be the time in London, UK?
questions.solution:
New Delhi operates on Indian Standard Time (IST), which is UTC +5:30. London operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) during winter or British Summer Time (BST, which is GMT +1) during summer. To determine the time in London, UK, when it is 12 noon in New Delhi, you need to adjust for the time difference.

During Winter (GMT):

New Delhi is 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of GMT.

If it's 12 noon in New Delhi, it would be 12:00 noon - 5:30 hours = 6:30 A.M. in London.

During Summer (BST):

New Delhi is 4 hours and 30 minutes ahead of BST.

If it's 12 noon in New Delhi, it would be 12:00 noon - 4:30 hours = 7:30 A.M. in London.

If we consider the typical time difference during most of the year, the correct option is:

Option A: 6:30 A.M.

Note that this answer assumes the scenario is during the winter months when London is on GMT.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
questions.question:
Which of the following positions of Sun, Earth and Moon is/are suitable for Spring Tide?

1. SYZYGY Conjunction

2. SYZYGY Opposition

3. Quadrature

Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
Spring tides occur when the Sun, Earth, and Moon are aligned. This alignment happens in two scenarios: during a new moon (syzygy conjunction) and during a full moon (syzygy opposition). Quadrature refers to the configuration where the Sun and Moon are at right angles with respect to the Earth, which results in neap tides, not spring tides.

Therefore, the positions suitable for spring tides are:

SYZYGY Conjunction

SYZYGY Opposition

The correct answer is:

Option C: 1 and 2

questions.question: Which one of the following tribes from India's North-East had the earliest known association with cultivation and production of tea?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Singphos.

Singpho Tribe of Northeast India:

  • The Singpho tribe, located in Assam and Arunachal Pradesh, has a long history with tea cultivation and production.

  • They were cultivating tea long before the British started commercial tea production in Assam.

  • The tribe traditionally made a type of tea known as "phalap," which is prepared by boiling the tea leaves.

Here's why the other options are incorrect:

  • Khasis: The Khasi people are an ethnic group from Meghalaya in north-eastern India, with significant populations in Assam and parts of Bangladesh. They are not historically associated with the earliest tea cultivation and production.

  • Garos: The Garo people are a Tibeto-Burmese ethnic group living mostly in the northeastern Indian state of Meghalaya, with a smaller population in Bangladesh. They are not known for their early involvement in tea cultivation and production.

  • Jayantias: The Jaintia Kingdom was matriarchal in present-day Sylhet division of Bangladesh, Meghalaya state of India, and parts of Nagaon and Morigaon districts of Assam. While historically significant, they are not associated with the early cultivation and production of tea.
