questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following is the oldest era in Geological History determining the age of various rock types?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Precambrian.

  • The oldest era in Geological History is the Precambrian. This era extends from the formation of Earth around 4.6 billion years ago to the beginning of the Cambrian Period, approximately 541 million years ago.

  • The Precambrian is divided into three eons: Hadean, Archean, and Proterozoic.

  • Hadean Eon (4.6 - 4.0 billion years ago): This eon corresponds to the early stages of Earth's formation, characterized by intense heat, frequent collisions with celestial bodies, and the gradual cooling of the Earth's surface.

  • Archean Eon (4.0 - 2.5 billion years ago): During this eon, the Earth's crust began to solidify, and the first continents and oceans formed. Primitive life forms, such as bacteria and archaea, appeared in the latter part of the Archean.

  • Proterozoic Eon (2.5 billion - 541 million years ago): This eon is marked by the emergence of more complex life forms, including multicellular organisms. Oxygen levels in the atmosphere increased due to photosynthetic processes carried out by cyanobacteria. Towards the end of the Proterozoic, the first complex animals evolved.

  • The Precambrian sets the stage for the subsequent geological eras, providing the foundation for the diverse life forms that would flourish in the Paleozoic, Mesozoic, and Cenozoic eras.

  • It represents an immense span of time, encompassing the majority of Earth's history before the explosion of complex life during the Cambrian Period.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question:
A geographical grid has:

1. Lines that run east to west around the globe to mark latitude

2. Lines that run north to south from north pole to south pole indicate longitude

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
A geographical grid is a system used to locate positions on the Earth's surface. This grid includes lines of latitude and longitude.

The first statement is: "Lines that run east to west around the globe to mark latitude."

This statement is correct. Lines of latitude, also known as parallels, run in an east-west direction around the globe and are used to measure the distance north or south of the equator.

The second statement is: "Lines that run north to south from north pole to south pole indicate longitude."

This statement is also correct. Lines of longitude, also known as meridians, run in a north-south direction and are used to measure the distance east or west of the Prime Meridian.

Given that both statements are correct, the correct answer is:

Option C: Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: In which one of the following layers of the atmosphere all weather phenomenon occur?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Troposphere. Here's why:

The troposphere is the lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere, extending from the surface to about 7-15 kilometers (4-9 miles) in altitude. It's where we live and where all weather phenomena occur, including:

  • Clouds: Formed by the condensation of water vapor.
  • Precipitation: Rain, snow, sleet, and hail.
  • Wind: Caused by differences in air pressure.
  • Temperature changes: The troposphere experiences most of the daily temperature fluctuations.

Here's why the other options are incorrect:

  • Mesosphere: This layer is above the troposphere and is characterized by decreasing temperatures. It's too cold and thin for weather as we know it.
  • Thermosphere: This is the hottest layer of the atmosphere, but it's extremely thin. It contains the ionosphere, which is important for radio communication, but doesn't experience weather.
  • Stratosphere: This layer contains the ozone layer, which protects us from harmful UV radiation. It's very stable and doesn't experience significant weather changes.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: In which one of the following states 'Roopkund' lake is located?
questions.solution:
Roopkund Lake, often referred to as the 'Skeleton Lake' due to the hundreds of ancient human skeletons found at its edge, is located in the state of Uttarakhand.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option D: Uttarakhand

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following soils is ideal for growing cotton?
questions.solution:
The ideal soil for growing cotton is "Regur soil." Regur soil is also popularly known as Black Cotton Soil due to its suitability for cotton cultivation. Let's explore why Regur soil is the best option for growing cotton:

Characteristics of Regur Soil:

1. High Moisture Retention: Regur soil has excellent moisture-retaining capacity. This characteristic ensures that the cotton plants receive adequate water even during dry spells, which is crucial for their growth.

2. Rich in Minerals: This type of soil is rich in essential minerals like calcium carbonate, magnesium, potash, and lime. These minerals provide the necessary nutrients that support the healthy growth of cotton plants.

3. Depth and Fertility: Regur soil is deep and fertile, which allows for the extensive root development of cotton plants. The deep roots can access nutrients and moisture from the soil more effectively.

4. Water Drainage: While the soil can retain moisture, it also has good drainage properties, preventing waterlogging, which can be harmful to cotton plants.

5. Type of Soil: Cotton thrives well in clayey loam to loamy soil with good aeration, and Regur soil provides just the right structure.

Considering these factors, the most suitable or ideal soil from the given options for growing cotton is:

Option A: Regur soil

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Where is Central Institute of Buddhist Studies situated?
questions.solution:
The Central Institute of Buddhist Studies is situated in Leh, which is in the Union Territory of Ladakh, India. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: Leh

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about physical features of India:

1. The southern point of the Peninsular Plateau is formed by the Nilgiri Hills where the Eastern and the Western Ghats meet

2. The Cardamom Hills may be regarded as a continuation of the Eastern Ghats

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement to determine its correctness regarding the physical features of India.

Statement 1: The southern point of the Peninsular Plateau is formed by the Nilgiri Hills where the Eastern and the Western Ghats meet.

This statement is largely correct. The Nilgiri Hills, also known as the "Blue Mountains," are indeed a range of mountains in the western part of Tamil Nadu state, at the junction of the Eastern and Western Ghats. Hence, the Nilgiri Hills do form a significant part of the southern point of the Peninsular Plateau.

Statement 2: The Cardamom Hills may be regarded as a continuation of the Eastern Ghats.

This statement is incorrect. The Cardamom Hills are actually a part of the Southern Western Ghats located in Kerala and Tamil Nadu. They are not a continuation of the Eastern Ghats. The Eastern Ghats are a discontinuous range of mountains running along India’s eastern coast, while the Cardamom Hills are part of the Western Ghats which are more continuous and run along the western coast.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: 1 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following is the narrow belt parallel to the Shiwalik foothills where streams and rivers coming from the mountains deposit heavy materials of rocks and boulders?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Bhabar. Here's why:

The Bhabar is a narrow, porous belt situated parallel to the Shiwalik foothills. It's characterized by the deposition of heavy materials like rocks and boulders carried down by rivers and streams originating in the Himalayas. This deposition occurs as the rivers emerge from the mountains and lose their erosive power due to the gentler slope.

Let's understand why the other options are incorrect:

Option A: Tarai - The Tarai is a marshy, fertile belt located south of the Bhabar. It's formed by the groundwater that seeps out from the Bhabar, creating a waterlogged environment suitable for agriculture.

Option B: Bhangar - Bhangar is an older, higher alluvial plain situated further away from the rivers. It's characterized by a higher concentration of kankar (calcium carbonate nodules) and is generally less fertile than the Khadar.

Option D: Khadar - Khadar refers to the newer, lower alluvial plain located closer to the rivers. It receives fresh alluvial deposits during floods, making it the most fertile part of the Indo-Gangetic Plain.

Therefore, the Bhabar is the correct answer as it directly corresponds to the description of the narrow belt where heavy materials are deposited by rivers and streams coming from the mountains.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question:
Which of the following statements about Suez Canal is/are correct?

1. Suez is a man-made navigation canal

2. Suez links Mediterranean Sea and the Red Sea

3. The economic significance of Suez Canal is less than that of Panama Canal

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: 1 and 2 only.

Let's analyze each statement:

Statement 1: Suez is a man-made navigation canal

This statement is correct. The Suez Canal was constructed in the 19th century, connecting the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, creating a crucial waterway for international shipping.

Statement 2: Suez links Mediterranean Sea and the Red Sea

This statement is also correct. As mentioned earlier, the primary function of the Suez Canal is to connect the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, facilitating maritime trade and transportation between Europe, Asia, and Africa.

Statement 3: The economic significance of Suez Canal is less than that of Panama Canal

This statement is incorrect. The Suez Canal holds immense economic significance, rivaling and even exceeding the Panama Canal in certain aspects. It facilitates the transportation of significant volumes of oil and other goods, directly impacting global trade and economic activity.

In conclusion, statements 1 and 2 are accurate, while statement 3 is false. Therefore, Option B is the correct answer.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following is NOT an example of Fold Mountain?
questions.solution:
Fold mountains are mountain ranges that are formed primarily by the effects of folding on layers within the upper part of the Earth's crust. Fold mountains are typically formed at convergent plate boundaries where tectonic plates collide, causing the rock layers to be compressed and pushed upwards. Examples of fold mountains include the Alps, the Andes, and the Rockies. However, Mt. Fuji is not a fold mountain.

Mt. Fuji is a stratovolcano, also known as a composite volcano, which is formed from multiple layers of hardened lava, tephra, pumice, and volcanic ash. Therefore, it does not belong to the category of fold mountains.

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option A: Mt. Fuji

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following is an organic soil?
questions.solution:
Among the given options, the correct answer for an organic soil type is:

Option B: Histosols

Histosols are organic soils that form in wetland conditions where the accumulation of organic material exceeds the rate of decomposition. These soils are rich in organic matter and are commonly found in bogs, marshes, or other areas with a high-water table. They are characterized by a high content of organic carbon, generally greater than 20-30%, and have a thick layer of organic soil material that is often referred to as peat or muck.

Here's a brief explanation about the other options:

  • Aridisols: These are soils found in arid environments and are characterized by a lack of moisture. They are typically sandy or loamy and have limited organic material.

  • Oxisols: These are heavily weathered tropical soils with a high content of iron and aluminum oxides. They are low in nutrients and organic matter.

  • Vertisols: These soils contain a high percentage of expanding clay minerals, which cause them to swell when wet and shrink upon drying. They are not known for high organic material content.

Thus, Option B (Histosols) is the one that represents an organic soil.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: The North-south and East-west Road Corridors of India cross each other at which of the following cities?
questions.solution:
The North-South and East-West Road Corridors of India are significant routes that facilitate transportation across the country. The North-South Corridor spans from Srinagar in the north to Kanyakumari in the south, whereas the East-West Corridor extends from Silchar in the east to Porbandar in the west.

When these two major corridors intersect, they do so at an important convergence point. Among the given options, the city where both corridors intersect is:

Option D: Jhansi

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following climatic regions of the world have a typical characteristic of seasonal reversal of wind?
questions.solution:
The climatic region with a typical characteristic of seasonal reversal of wind is the Monsoon Climate.

Monsoon Climate, primarily observed in regions like South Asia, Southeast Asia, and parts of East Asia, is characterized by a dramatic seasonal change in wind direction. During the summer months, the land heats up much more quickly than the ocean. This causes a low-pressure area over the land and a high-pressure area over the ocean, leading to moist oceanic air being drawn inland, bringing intense rainfall. In the winter months, the scenario reverses: the land cools faster than the ocean, resulting in a high-pressure area over the land and a low-pressure area over the ocean. Consequently, dry and cooler air from the land flows out towards the ocean.

This seasonal reversal in wind direction is a defining characteristic of the Monsoon Climate.

The other options listed do not exhibit this phenomenon:

British type Climate (Option A) is characterized by mild temperatures and precipitation throughout the year, influenced primarily by westerly winds and maritime influences.

Mediterranean Climate (Option B) features hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters, primarily influenced by the seasonal shifts in the subtropical high-pressure belts and westerly winds, but without a pronounced reversal of wind direction.

China type Climate (Option C), also known as the humid subtropical climate, has hot, humid summers and mild to cool winters, influenced by monsoon-like patterns, but not as pronounced as the Monsoon Climate in terms of seasonal wind reversal.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: Monsoon Climate

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following statement is NOT correct?
options: Bokaro steel plant is located in Damodar river basin
Jamshedpur steel plant is located on the confluence of Damodar and Barakar
Salem steel plant is located on the bank of Kaveri River
Bhilai steel plant obtains water from Tandula reservoir
questions.solution:
To determine which of the statements is not correct, we need to examine the specific locations and characteristics of these steel plants in relation to the bodies of water mentioned.

Option A: Bokaro steel plant is located in Damodar river basin.

This statement is correct. The Bokaro steel plant is indeed situated in the Damodar river basin in the state of Jharkhand, India.

Option B: Jamshedpur steel plant is located on the confluence of Damodar and Barakar.

This statement is incorrect. The Jamshedpur steel plant, also known as Tata Steel, is located near the confluence of the Subarnarekha and Kharkai rivers, not the Damodar and Barakar rivers.

Option C: Salem steel plant is located on the bank of Kaveri River.

This statement is correct. The Salem steel plant is located in Tamil Nadu, near the Kaveri River.

Option D: Bhilai steel plant obtains water from Tandula reservoir.

This statement is correct. The Bhilai steel plant in Chhattisgarh obtains water from the Tandula reservoir.

Based on the evaluation of each option, the correct answer is:

Option B: Jamshedpur steel plant is located on the confluence of Damodar and Barakar.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following crops is generally benefitted by the rain caused by western disturbances in India?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Wheat.

Western disturbances are weather systems that originate over the Mediterranean Sea and travel eastwards, bringing moisture-laden winds to the Indian subcontinent. These disturbances are more prominent during the winter months. When they reach the northwestern parts of India, they cause winter rainfall.

This winter rainfall is crucial for the Rabi crop season, particularly for wheat cultivation. Wheat is a staple food grain in North India and is generally sown in October-November and harvested in March-April. The rainfall provided by western disturbances helps in maintaining the soil moisture, which is beneficial for the wheat crop during its growth stages.

On the contrary, the other crops listed—rice, bajra, and cotton—have different growing seasons and climatic requirements:

  • Rice is primarily a Kharif crop, grown during the monsoon season (June to September) and requires substantial water.
  • Bajra (pearl millet) is also a Kharif crop that thrives in arid and semi-arid regions with minimal moisture requirement.
  • Cotton, a Kharif crop as well, needs hot and sunny conditions for optimal growth.

Therefore, the western disturbances' rainfall is particularly beneficial for wheat, enhancing its yield and ensuring a good harvest.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one of the following pairs of city and industry is NOT correctly matched?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D:

Raniganj: Iron and steel industry

This pair is NOT correctly matched. Here’s an explanation for each of the pairs:

Option A: Saharanpur : Paper industry

This is correctly matched. Saharanpur, a city in Uttar Pradesh, is well-known for its paper industry, along with its wood carving industry.

Option B: Chanderi : Cotton textile industry

This is correctly matched. Chanderi, located in Madhya Pradesh, is famous for its traditional Chanderi sarees, which are a product of the local cotton textile industry.

Option C: Bhadohi : Carpet industry

This is correctly matched. Bhadohi, often referred to as the "Carpet City," is renowned for its carpet-weaving industry, which is a major contributor to the local economy.

Option D: Raniganj : Iron and steel industry

This is NOT correctly matched. Raniganj, located in West Bengal, is primarily known for its coal mining industry rather than iron and steel. The major steel plants in India are located in places like Jamshedpur, Bhilai, and Rourkela.

Therefore, Option D is the correct answer as it does not correctly match the city with its prominent industry.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: Which one among the following states was NOT impacted by the Land Boundary Agreement Treaty India and Bangladesh between (2015)?
questions.solution:
The Land Boundary Agreement (LBA) between India and Bangladesh, signed in 2015, primarily impacted the boundary demarcation issues between the two countries. This agreement aimed to resolve long-standing border disputes and to exchange enclaves between the nations, thereby simplifying the international boundary and aiding in better administration.

The Indian states affected by the LBA were primarily those that shared a land border with Bangladesh. These states include West Bengal, Assam, Meghalaya, and Tripura. The agreement saw the exchange of enclaves, simplifying the border in these regions.

Given this context, the state that was NOT impacted by the Land Boundary Agreement Treaty between India and Bangladesh in 2015 is Arunachal Pradesh. This state does not share a border with Bangladesh and thus was not involved in the boundary adjustments dictated by the treaty.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: Arunachal Pradesh

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: The 480 MW Phukot Karnali Hydro Electric Project is situated in
questions.solution:
The 480 MW Phukot Karnali Hydro Electric Project is situated in Nepal.

Let's break down this information a bit further:

Phukot Karnali Hydro Electric Project: This is a hydroelectric project, meaning it generates electricity by using the energy of flowing or falling water. Hydroelectric projects are a vital source of renewable energy and play a significant role in providing sustainable power to regions.

480 MW: MW stands for megawatt, a unit of power. 480 MW represents the capacity of the power plant, which is a substantial amount of energy. To give you an idea, 1 MW can power approximately 1,000 homes, so a 480 MW plant could theoretically power around 480,000 homes.

Nepal: Nepal is a country located in South Asia, sharing borders with China and India. It is renowned for its mountainous topography, including the Himalayas, which are home to ten of the world's fourteen highest peaks, including Mount Everest. The country has significant potential for hydroelectric power due to its numerous rivers and the steep gradients provided by the mountainous terrain.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about a river:

This river originates in the Hindu Kush mountain range and flows into Hamoun wetlands after covering a long stretch of about 1,100 km. There has been serious conflicts between two neighbouring nations regarding the sharing of water from this river. Identify the river from the following:
questions.solution:
The river described in the statement is the Helmand River. Here’s how we can identify it based on the provided information:

The Helmand River originates in the Hindu Kush mountain range in Afghanistan. It is about 1,100 km long and flows into the Hamoun wetlands, which are located in the Sistan Basin straddling the Afghanistan-Iran border. This river has been a source of longstanding disputes between Afghanistan and Iran concerning water sharing.

Let’s go through the rest of the options for clarity:

Option A: Mekong

The Mekong River originates in the Tibetan Plateau and flows through several countries in Southeast Asia, including China, Myanmar, Laos, Thailand, Cambodia, and Vietnam. It eventually empties into the South China Sea. It doesn't fit the description provided.

Option B: Tarim

The Tarim River flows through the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region of China. It does not fit the origin (Hindu Kush) or the ending point (Hamoun wetlands) mentioned in the statement.

Option C: Indus

The Indus River originates in the Tibetan Plateau and flows through China, India, and Pakistan, eventually emptying into the Arabian Sea. While it passes near the Hindu Kush, it doesn't end in the Hamoun wetlands.

Option D: Helmand

The Helmand River, however, satisfies all conditions described: it starts in the Hindu Kush mountain range, covers a distance of about 1,100 km, and flows into the Hamoun wetlands. This river is also known for the water disputes between Afghanistan and Iran, making it the correct answer.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: Helmand

questions.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
questions.question: The Nova Kakhovka dam, which was in news recently, is situated in
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C, Ukraine.

The Nova Kakhovka dam is located on the Dnieper River in southern Ukraine. It is situated near the city of Nova Kakhovka in the Kherson Oblast. The dam has been a focal point of the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine, as its destruction has had significant consequences for both countries.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
(a) (i) Draw a ray diagram to show the working of a compound microscope. Obtain the expression for the total magnification for the final image to be formed at the near point.

(ii) In a compound microscope an object is placed at a distance of $$1.5 \mathrm{~cm}$$ from the objective of focal length $$1.25 \mathrm{~cm}$$. If the eye-piece has a focal length of $$5 \mathrm{~cm}$$ and the final image is formed at the near point, find the magnifying power of the microscope.

OR

(b) (i) Draw a ray diagram for the formation of image of an object by an astronomical telescope, in normal adjustment. Obtain the expression for its magnifying power.

(ii) The magnifying power of an astronomical telescope in normal adjustment is 2.9 and the objective and the eyepiece are separated by a distance of $$150 \mathrm{~cm}$$. Find the focal lengths of the two lenses.
questions.solution:
(a) (i) Ray diagram of compound microscope:

In a compound microscope there are two lenses objective $$(\mathrm{O})$$ and Eyepiece (E).

Object PQ is placed in front of the objective at a distance more than the focal length of the objective.

An inverted, magnified, real image $$P_1 Q_1$$ is formed in front of eyepiece within the optical centre and the focus of the eyepiece. This acts as the object of the eyepiece. An (erect with respect to $$\mathrm{P}_1 \mathrm{Q}_1$$, inverted with respect to $$P Q$$ ), magnified, virtual image $$P_2 Q_2$$ is formed at a distance $$\mathrm{D}$$ (minimum distance of distinct vision) from the eyepiece.

For objective:

Object distance = u

Image distance = v

$$\text { Magnification }=m_{\mathrm{o}}=\frac{v}{u}=\frac{P_1 Q_1}{P Q}$$

Applying the lens formula,

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{-u}=\frac{1}{f_0} \\ & \text { Or, } \quad 1+\frac{v}{u}=\frac{v}{f_0} \\ & \text { Or, } \quad \frac{v}{u}=\frac{v}{f_0}-1 \\ \end{aligned}$$

For eyepiece:

$$\text { Magnification }=m_e=1+\frac{D}{f_e}$$

Magnification of the combination of objective and eyepiece $$=m=m_O \times m_e$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{Or,} \quad & m=\frac{v}{u} \times\left(1+\frac{D}{f e}\right) \\ \text{Or,} \quad & m=\left(\frac{v}{f_0}-1\right)\left(1+\frac{D}{f e}\right) \end{aligned}$$

$$\mathrm{P}_1 \mathrm{Q}_1$$ image is formed very close to the eyepiece, hence $$v$$ can be approximated as the distance between the two lenses i.e., the length of the tube(L).

$$\therefore \quad m=\left(\frac{L}{f_0}-1\right)\left(1+\frac{D}{f_e}\right)$$

Since, $$f_e < D$$ and $$f_0 < L$$, hence the above expression may be approximated as,

$$m=\frac{L}{f_0} \times \frac{D}{f_e}$$

(ii) Given, $$u=1.5 \mathrm{~cm}, f_0=1.25 \mathrm{~cm}, f_e=5 \mathrm{~cm}, D =25 \mathrm{~cm}$$

Here, all alphabets are in their usual meanings

Applying lens formula for objective lens,

$$\begin{aligned} \frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{u} & =\frac{1}{f_0} \\ \text { Or, } \quad \frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{-1.5} & =\frac{1}{1.25} \\ \therefore \quad v & =7.5 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text { Magnification } & =m=\frac{v}{u} \times\left(1+\frac{D}{f_e}\right) \\ \text { Or, } \quad m & =\frac{7.5}{-1.5} \times\left(1+\frac{25}{5}\right) \\ \therefore \quad m & =-30 \end{aligned}$$

OR

(b) (i) Astronomical telescope in normal adjustment:

In an astronomical telescope there are two lenses - objective $$(\mathrm{O})$$ and Eyepiece (E).

The two lenses are so placed during focussing that the foci of the lenses meet at a point.

Objective is directed towards the object at infinity.

Parallel rays coming from the object meet at the focus of the objective and forms an inverted, real image $$\mathrm{P}_1 \mathrm{Q}_1$$ in front of eyepiece. This point is the focus of eyepiece too.

This acts as the object of the eyepiece. An (inverted with respect to $$P_1 Q_1$$, erect with respect to original object), highly magnified, real image is formed at infinity.

Magnification = m

$$=\frac{\text { Angle subtended at eye by the final image }}{\text { Angle subtended at eye by the object }}$$

Or, $$m=$$ $$=\frac{\text { Angle subtended at eyepiece by the final image }}{\text { Angle subtended at objective by the object }}$$

Or, $$m=\frac{\beta}{\alpha}$$

Or, $$m=\frac{\angle Q_1 E P_1}{\angle Q_1 O P_1}$$

Or, $$m=\frac{\tan \angle Q_1 E P_1}{\tan \angle Q_1 O P_1}$$

$$\text { [ } \alpha \text { and } \beta \text { being very small, } \tan \alpha=\alpha \text { and } \tan \beta=\beta \text { ] }$$

Or, $$m=\frac{\frac{Q_1 P_1}{Q_1 E}}{\frac{Q_1 P_1}{Q_1 O}}$$

Or, $$m=\frac{Q_1 O}{Q_1 E}$$

$$\therefore \quad m=\frac{f_0}{f_e}$$

(ii) Since, $$m=\frac{f_0}{f_e}$$

Or, $$2.9=\frac{f_0}{f_e}$$

$$\therefore \quad f_o=2.9 f_e$$

Also, $$f_o+f_e=150$$

Or, $$2.9 f_e+f_e=150$$

$$\therefore \quad f_e=\frac{150}{3.9}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =38.46 \mathrm{~cm} \\\\ f_O & =2.9 f_e \\\\ & =2.9 \times 38.46 \\\\ & =111.54 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
A lens is a transparent optical medium bounded by two surfaces; at least one of which should be spherical. Considering image formation by a single spherical surface successively at the two surfaces of a lens, lens maker's formula is obtained. It is useful to design lenses of desired focal length using surfaces of suitable radii of curvature. This formula helps us obtain a relation between $$u, v$$ and $$f$$ for a lens. Lenses form images of objects and they are used in a number of optical devices, for example microscopes and telescopes.

(i) An object AB is kept in front of a composite convex lens, as shown in figure. Will the lens produce one image? If not, explain.

(ii) A real image of an object formed by a convex lens is observed on a screen. If the screen is removed, will the image still be formed? Explain.

(iii) A double convex lens is made of glass of refractive index 1.55 with both faces of the same radius of curvature. Find the radius of curvature required if focal length is $$20 \mathrm{~cm}$$.

OR

(iii) Two convex lenses A and B of focal lengths $$15 \mathrm{~cm}$$ and $$10 \mathrm{~cm}$$ respectively are placed coaxially '$$d$$' distance apart. A point object is kept at a distance of $$30 \mathrm{~cm}$$ in front of lens A. Find the value of '$$d$$' so that the rays emerging from lens $B$ are parallel to its principal axis.
questions.solution:
(i) No, the lens will not produce one image. It will produce two images. Refractive index upper and lower section of the lens being different there will be two foci and hence, two images will be formed.

(ii) If the screen is removed, the image will be still formed and it can be seen by looking through the lens through the specific cone of light.

(iii) Using lens maker's formula

$$\frac{1}{f}=(\mu-1)\left(\frac{1}{r_1}-\frac{1}{r_2}\right)$$

Or, $$\frac{1}{20}=(1.55-1)\left(\frac{2}{r}\right)$$

$$\therefore \quad r=22$$ cm

OR

The equivalent focal length of two lenses separated by a distance $$d$$ be $$f$$

$$\begin{aligned} \frac{1}{f} & =\frac{1}{f_1}+\frac{1}{f_2}-\frac{d}{f_1 f_2} \\\\ \text { Or, } \quad \frac{1}{f} & =\frac{1}{15}+\frac{1}{10}-\frac{d}{15 \times 10} \\\\ \text { Or, } \quad \frac{1}{f} & =\frac{1}{6}-\frac{d}{150} \end{aligned}$$

When a point object is kept distance $$30 \mathrm{~cm}$$ from this lens combination, the rays emerge as parallel to the principal axis. So, focal length of the combination is $$30 \mathrm{~cm}$$.

$$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \frac{1}{30}=\frac{1}{6}-\frac{d}{150} \\\\ & \text { Or, } \quad \frac{d}{150}=\frac{1}{6}-\frac{1}{30} \\\\ & \therefore \quad d=20 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: A ray of monochromatic light propagating in air, is incident on the surface of water. Which of the following will be the same for the reflected and refracted rays?

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: In the given figure the radius of curvature of curved face in the plano-convex and the plano-concave lens is $$15 \mathrm{~cm}$$ each. The refractive index of the material of the lenses is 1.5. Find the final position of the image formed.
questions.solution:
In the given figure, the radius of curvature for the curved face in both the plano-convex and the plano-concave lenses is 15 cm each. The refractive index of the lens material is 1.5. We need to determine the final position of the formed image.

Focal Length of the Plano-Convex Lens

Let the focal length of the plano-convex lens be $ f_1 $:

$ \frac{1}{f_1} = (1.5 - 1) \left(\frac{1}{15} - \frac{1}{\infty}\right) $

Simplifying:

$ \frac{1}{f_1} = \frac{1}{30} $

Thus,

$ f_1 = 30 \, \text{cm} $

Focal Length of the Plano-Concave Lens

Let the focal length of the plano-concave lens be $ f_2 $:

$ \frac{1}{f_2} = (1.5 - 1) \left(\frac{1}{\infty} - \frac{1}{15}\right) $

Thus,

$ \frac{1}{f_2} = -\frac{1}{30} $

So,

$ f_2 = -30 \, \text{cm} $

Position of the Image

Since parallel rays are incident on the plano-convex lens, it will form an image at a distance of 30 cm from the lens (since its focal length is 30 cm). This image will act as an object for the plano-concave lens.

Object Distance for Plano-Concave Lens

$ \text{Object distance} = 30 \, \text{cm} - 20 \, \text{cm} = 10 \, \text{cm} $

Applying the Lens Formula for the Plano-Concave Lens

$ \frac{1}{v_2} - \frac{1}{u_2} = \frac{1}{f_2} $

Substitute the known values:

$ \frac{1}{v_2} - \frac{1}{10} = -\frac{1}{30} $

Solving for $ v_2 $:

$ \frac{1}{v_2} = \frac{1}{10} - \frac{1}{30} $

$ \frac{1}{v_2} = \frac{3 - 1}{30} = \frac{2}{30} = \frac{1}{15} $

Thus,

$ v_2 = 15 \, \text{cm} $

The final image is formed 15 cm from the plano-concave lens.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics Outside Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: Define critical angle for a given pair of media and total internal reflection. Obtain the relation between the critical angle and refractive index of the medium.
questions.solution:
Critical angle :

When light refracts from a denser to a rarer medium, the angle of incidence for which the angle of refraction is $$90^{\circ}$$ is known as the critical angle.

Total internal reflection :

When light refracts from a denser to a rarer medium, if the angle of incidence is greater than the critical angle, the light is reflected back to the denser medium. This phenomenon is known as total internal reflection.

Relation between critical angle and refractive index :

Refractive index of denser medium $$=\mu_{\text {denser }}$$

Refractive index of rarer medium $$=\mu_{\text {rarer }}$$

Critical angle $$=\theta_c$$

$$\therefore$$ denser $$\mu_{\text {rarer }}=\frac{\sin \theta_c}{\sin 90^{\circ}}$$

$$\frac{\mu_{\text {rarer }}}{\mu_{\text {denser }}}=\sin \theta_c$$

or, $$\frac{\mu_{\text {denser }}}{\mu_{\text {rarer }}}=\frac{1}{\sin \theta_c}$$

If the rarer medium is air, then $$\mu_{\text {rarer }}=1$$

$$\therefore \mu=\frac{1}{\sin \theta_c}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A plane polarized blue light ray is incident on a prism such that there is no reflection from the surface of the prism. The angle of deviation of the emergent ray is $\delta=60^{\circ}$ (see Figure-1). The angle of minimum deviation for red light from the same prism is $\delta_{\min }=30^{\circ}$ (see Figure-2). The refractive index of the prism material for blue light is $\sqrt{3}$. Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The blue light is polarized in the plane of incidence.
The angle of the prism is $45^{\circ}$.
The refractive index of the material of the prism for red light is $\sqrt{2}$.
The angle of refraction for blue light in air at the exit plane of the prism is $60^{\circ}$.
questions.solution:
When light strikes a surface at Brewster's angle, it becomes polarized perpendicular to the plane of incidence.

Therefore, there is no reflection of blue light observed.

This implies that the light must be polarized within the plane of incidence.



$\begin{aligned} \tan i & =\sqrt{3} \\\\ i & =60^{\circ} \\\\ \delta & =i+e-A \\\\ 60 & =60+e-A \\\\ \Rightarrow A & =e\end{aligned}$

$\begin{aligned} \frac{\sin i}{\sin r_1} & =\sqrt{3} \\\\ \Rightarrow \frac{\sin 60}{\sin r_1} & =\sqrt{3} \Rightarrow r_1=30^{\circ}\end{aligned}$

$\begin{array}{rlrl}r_2 =A-r_1 \\\\ r \sin \left(A-r_1\right) =\sin e \\\\ \Rightarrow \sqrt{3} \sin (A-30) =\sin A \\\\ r_3\left[\sin A \cos 30^{\circ}-\cos A\right. \sin 30]=\sin A \\\\ \Rightarrow \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}(\sqrt{3}-\cot A) =1 \\\\ \Rightarrow \cot A =\sqrt{3}-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\\\ \Rightarrow \cot A =\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\\\ \Rightarrow A =60^{\circ}\end{array}$

So, $\quad e=60^{\circ}$ (for blue)

For red,

$$ \begin{aligned} \mu & =\frac{\sin \left(\frac{\delta_m+A}{2}\right)}{\sin \frac{A}{2}} \\\\ & =\frac{\sin \left(\frac{30+60}{2}\right)}{\sin \frac{60}{2}}=\frac{\sin 45}{\sin 30}=\frac{1 / \sqrt{2}}{1 / 2} \\\\ \mu_{\text {red }} & =\sqrt{2} \end{aligned} $$

(A), (C), (D) are correct.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: An optical arrangement consists of two concave mirrors $M_1$ and $M_2$, and a convex lens $L$ with a common principal axis, as shown in the figure. The focal length of $\mathrm{L}$ is $10 \mathrm{~cm}$. The radii of curvature of $M_1$ and $M_2$ are $20 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $24 \mathrm{~cm}$, respectively. The distance between $L$ and $M_2$ is $20 \mathrm{~cm}$. A point object $S$ is placed at the mid-point between $\mathrm{L}$ and $M_2$ on the axis. When the distance between $\mathrm{L}$ and $\mathrm{M}_1$ is $n / 7 \mathrm{~cm}$, one of the images coincides with $\mathrm{S}$. The value of $n$ is _______.
questions.solution:
Case-1 : if $\mathrm{I}^{\text {st }}$ refraction on lens :

Given that the object is positioned at the focal point, the resulting light rays will transition to a parallel alignment.

Upon the first reflection at Mirror $ M_1 $:
As the incoming light rays are parallel, the resultant image will be formed at the focal point.


$\mathrm{II}^{\text {nd }}$ refraction from $\mathrm{L}$ :-

$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{u}=-(\mathrm{d}-10) \\\\ & \mathrm{f}=10 \mathrm{~cm} \\\\ & \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}}-\frac{1}{\mu}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{f}} \\\\ & \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{~d}-10}=\frac{1}{10} \\\\ & \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}}=\frac{1}{10}-\frac{1}{(\mathrm{~d}-10)} .......(i) \end{aligned} $$

This $v$ will be object for $M_2$, and image should be at $10 \mathrm{~cm}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{\mu}+\frac{1}{v_1}=\frac{1}{f} \\\\ & -\frac{1}{(20-v)}-\frac{1}{10}=-\frac{1}{12} \\\\ & \frac{1}{12}-\frac{1}{10}=\frac{1}{20-v} \\\\ & -\frac{2}{120}=\frac{1}{20-v} \\\\ & 20-v=-60 \\\\ & v=80 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned} $$

From equation (i), we get

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{80}=\frac{1}{10}-\frac{1}{d-10} \\\\ & \frac{1}{d-10}=\frac{1}{10}-\frac{1}{80} \\\\ & \frac{1}{d-10}=\frac{80-10}{800}=\frac{70}{800} \\\\ & d-10=\frac{80}{7} \Rightarrow d=10+\frac{80}{7}=\frac{150}{7} \\\\ & n=150 \end{aligned} $$

Case-2 : If $1^{\text {st }}$ reflection on mirror $m_2$ :



For $\mathrm{m}_2$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{\mathrm{~V}_1}+\frac{1}{-10}=\frac{1}{-12} \\\\ & \mathrm{~V}_1=60 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned} $$

Then refraction on lens $\mathrm{L}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{u}_2=80 \mathrm{~cm} \\\\ & \frac{1}{\mathrm{~V}_2}-\frac{1}{-60}=\frac{1}{10} \\\\ & \mathrm{~V}_2=\frac{80}{7} \end{aligned} $$

Then reflection on $\mathrm{m}_2$

Either $V_2$ is at centre (normal incidence)

$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{d}-\frac{80}{7}=20 \\\\ & \mathrm{~d}=\frac{220}{7} \\\\ & \frac{\mathrm{n}}{7}=\frac{220}{7} \\\\ & \mathrm{n}=220 \end{aligned} $$

$V_2$ is at pole of $m_2$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{d}-\frac{80}{7}=0 \\\\ & \mathrm{~d}=\frac{80}{7} \\\\ & \frac{\mathrm{n}}{7}=\frac{80}{7} \\\\ & \mathrm{n}=80 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: A monochromatic light wave is incident normally on a glass slab of thickness $d$, as shown in the figure. The refractive index of the slab increases linearly from $n_1$ to $n_2$ over the height $h$. Which of the following statement(s) is(are) true about the light wave emerging out of the slab?
options: It will deflect up by an angle $\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\left(n_2^2-n_1^2\right) d}{2 h}\right]$.
It will deflect up by an angle $\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\left(n_2-n_1\right) d}{h}\right]$.
The deflection angle depends only on $\left(n_2-n_1\right)$ and not on the individual values of $n_1$ and $n_2$.
questions.solution:
At point A on one end of the slab, the wavefront takes a time $$ t $$ to reach the other end. The time $$ t $$ can be expressed as the ratio of the distance traveled, $$ d $$, to the speed of the wave in the medium, which is $$ \frac{c}{n_1} $$ (where $$ c $$ is the speed of light in vacuum and $$ n_1 $$ is the refractive index of the medium at A). Therefore, the time taken $$ t $$ is given by $$ t = \frac{d}{\frac{c}{n_1}} = \frac{n_1 d}{c} $$.

Simultaneously, consider the wavefront at point B, located in a different medium with a refractive index $$ n_2 $$. In the same time $$ t $$, the wavefront at B travels a distance $$ s_1 $$. The distance $$ s_1 $$ can be calculated as the product of the speed of light in the medium at B, $$ \frac{c}{n_2} $$, and the time $$ t $$, yielding $$ s_1 = \frac{c}{n_2} \times \frac{n_1 d}{c} = \frac{d n_1}{n_2} $$.

Once the wavefront at B reaches the end of the slab, an additional time $$ t_1 $$ elapses. The time $$ t_1 $$ can be determined by considering the remaining distance the wavefront must travel in the slab, which is $$ d - s_1 $$, and dividing it by the speed of light in the medium at B. This gives us $$ t_1 = \left(\frac{d - s_1}{c}\right) n_2 = \left(d - \frac{d n_1}{n_2}\right) \frac{n_2}{c} = \frac{d(n_2 - n_1)}{c} $$.

During this time $$ t_1 $$, the wavefront at A will travel a distance $$ s_2 $$. The distance $$ s_2 $$ is simply the speed of light in vacuum multiplied by the time $$ t_1 $$, leading to $$ s_2 = c t_1 = d(n_2 - n_1) $$.

Finally, the angle $$ \theta $$ can be determined using the tangent function, where $$ \tan \theta $$ is the ratio of the difference in distances traveled by the wavefronts in the two media to the height $$ h $$ of the slab.

Therefore, $$ \tan \theta = \frac{(n_2 - n_1) d}{h} $$.

questions.title: BITSAT 2023
questions.question:
Select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active voice.

Did everybody miss the first bus?
options: The first bus was missed by everybody?
Was the first bus missed by everybody?
Had the first bus been missed by everybody?
questions.solution:
The given sentence "Did everybody miss the first bus?" is in active voice. To change it into passive voice, we need to make the object of the verb (the first bus) the subject of the sentence, and use the appropriate form of the verb "to be" along with the past participle of the main verb (miss).

Let's analyze the options:

Option A: The first bus was missed by everybody?

This option is in passive voice, but the sentence structure is incorrect. It should be a question, which requires an inversion of the subject and auxiliary verb.

Option B: Was the first bus missed by everybody?

This option is correct. It correctly inverts the auxiliary verb "was" and the subject "the first bus," making it a proper question in passive voice.

Option C: Everybody missed the first bus?

This option is in active voice and is essentially restating the original sentence without the question format being clear.

Option D: Had the first bus been missed by everybody?

This option is in passive voice, but it changes the tense to past perfect, which doesn't match the original simple past tense of the sentence.

Therefore, the correct option that best expresses the same sentence in passive voice is:

Option B: Was the first bus missed by everybody?

questions.title: BITSAT 2023
questions.question: A man of mass $m$ starts falling towards a planet of mass $$M$$ and radius $$R$$. As he reaches near to the surface, he realizes that will pass through a small role in the planet. As he enters the role, we sees that the planet is really made of two pieces a spherical shell of negligible thickness of mass $$3 M / 4$$ and a point mass $$M / 4$$ at the centre. Change in the force of gravity experienced by the man is
questions.solution:
When the man is outside the planet and close to its surface, the gravitational force is determined by the entire mass of the planet, $$M$$. The force of gravity at the surface can be given by Newton's law of gravitation:

$$ F = \frac{G M m}{R^2} $$

However, as the man passes through the hole in the planet, the gravitational force he experiences changes due to the unique mass distribution of the planet. The planet consists of a spherical shell of mass $$\frac{3M}{4}$$ and negligible thickness, and a point mass $$\frac{M}{4}$$ at the center.

Let's analyze the gravitational force when the man is inside the planet:

1. Spherical Shell Contribution:

A spherical shell of uniform density exerts no net gravitational force on a particle located inside it. Thus, the man inside the shell experiences no force from the spherical shell of mass $$\frac{3M}{4}$$.

2. Point Mass Contribution:

The point mass at the center, which is $$\frac{M}{4}$$, exerts a gravitational force on the man. According to Newton's law of gravitation, this force is given by:

$$ F' = \frac{G \left(\frac{M}{4}\right) m}{R^2} $$

To determine the change in the force of gravity experienced by the man, we subtract the force due to the point mass from the original force at the planet's surface:

Original force at the surface:

$$ F = \frac{G M m}{R^2} $$

Force inside due to point mass:

$$ F' = \frac{1}{4} \frac{G M m}{R^2} $$

Change in the force:

$$ \Delta F = F - F' $$

$$ \Delta F = \frac{G M m}{R^2} - \frac{1}{4} \frac{G M m}{R^2} $$

Simplifying the expression:

$$ \Delta F = \frac{G M m}{R^2} \left(1 - \frac{1}{4}\right) $$

$$ \Delta F = \frac{G M m}{R^2} \cdot \frac{3}{4} $$

$$ \Delta F = \frac{3}{4} \frac{G M m}{R^2} $$

Hence, the change in the force of gravity experienced by the man is

$$ \frac{3}{4} \frac{G M m}{R^2} $$

The correct answer is Option A: $$\frac{3}{4} \frac{G M m}{R^2}$$.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2023 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Two satellites $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{Q}$ are moving in different circular orbits around the Earth (radius $R$ ). The heights of $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{Q}$ from the Earth surface are $h_{\mathrm{P}}$ and $h_{\mathrm{Q}}$, respectively, where $h_{\mathrm{P}}=R / 3$. The accelerations of $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{Q}$ due to Earth's gravity are $g_{\mathrm{P}}$ and $g_{\mathrm{Q}}$, respectively. If $g_{\mathrm{P}} / g_{\mathrm{Q}}=36 / 25$, what is the value of $h_{\mathrm{Q}}$ ?
questions.solution:
The formula to calculate the acceleration due to gravity at a height ( h ) from the surface of the Earth is expressed as :

$ g = \frac{G M_e}{(R_e + h)^2} $

To find the ratio of gravitational acceleration at two different heights ( h_P ) and ( h_Q ) above the Earth's surface, use the formula for each and form a ratio :

$ \frac{g_P}{g_Q} = \frac{\frac{G M_e}{(R_e + h_P)^2}}{\frac{G M_e}{(R_e + h_Q)^2}} = \frac{(R_e + h_Q)^2}{(R_e + h_P)^2} $

Given $ h_P = \frac{R_e}{3} $, the ratio simplifies to:

$ \frac{g_P}{g_Q} = \frac{(R_e + h_Q)^2}{(R_e + \frac{R_e}{3})^2} = \frac{36}{25} $

Solving for $ h_Q $ in terms of $ R_e $ yields $ h_Q = \frac{3R_e}{5} $.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Chemistry Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question: Why is boiling point of o-dichlorobenzene higher than p-dichlorobenzene but melting point of para isomer is higher than ortho isomer?
questions.solution: Melting point of $$\mathbf{p}$$-Chlorobenzene is higher than o-Chlorobenzene because of its closed packed structure which have high intramolecular forces of attraction. That's why high amount of temperature is required to break down the bonds while boiling point of o-chlorobenzene is high because of high dipole interaction present at ortho-position. There is great dipole-dipole forces of attraction between carbon and chlorine atom which results into high boiling point as compared to p-chlorobenzene which have zero dipole moment.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Chemistry Delhi Set 1 - 2023
questions.question:
Read the passage carefully and answer the questions that follow :

Nucleophilic Substitution Nucleophilic Substitution reaction of haloalkane can be conducted according to both $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$ and $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$$ mechanisms. $$S_N 1$$ is a two step reaction while $$S_N 2$$ is a single step reaction. For any haloalkane which mechanism is followed depends on factors such as structure of haloalkane, properties of leaving group, nucleophilic reagent and solvent.

Influences of solvent polarity: In $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$ reaction, the polarity of the system increases from the reactant to the transition state, because a polar solvent has a greater effect on the transition state than the reactant, thereby reducing activation energy and accelerating the reaction. In $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$$ reaction, the polarity of the system generally does not change from the reactant to the transition state and only charge dispersion occurs. At this time, polar solvent has a great stabilizing effect on $$\mathrm{Nu}$$ than the transition state, thereby increasing activation energy and slow down the reaction rate. For example, the decomposition rate $$\left(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1\right)$$ of tertiary chlorobutane at $$25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ in water (dielectric constant 79) is 300000 times faster than in ethanol (dielectric constant 24 ). The reaction rate $$\left(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2\right)$$ of 2 -Bromopropane and $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ in ethanol containing $$40 \%$$ water is twice slower than in absolute ethanol. Hence the level of solvent polarity has influence on both $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$ and $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$$ reaction, but with different results. Generally speaking weak polar solvent is favourable for $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$$ reaction, while strong polar solvent is favourable for $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$. Generally speaking the substitution reaction of tertiary haloalkane is based on $$S_N 1$$ mechanism in solvents with a strong polarity (for example ethanol containing water).

Answer the following questions :

(a) Why racemisation occurs in $$S_N 1$$ ?

(b) Why is ethanol less polar than water?

(c) Which one of the following in each pair is more reactive towards $$S_N 2$$ reaction?

(i) $$\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{I} \text { or } \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{Cl}$$

(ii)

OR

(c) Arrange the following in the increasing order of their reactivity towards $$S_N 1$$ reactions:

(i) 2-Bromo-2-methylbutane, 1-Bromopentane, 2-Bromopentane

(ii) 1-Bromo-3-methylbutane, 2-Bromo-2methylbutane, 2-Bromo-3-methylbutane
questions.solution:
(a) In $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$ mechanism, there is intermediate carbo cation formed. Due to which recmization of the product take place or $$\mathrm{D}$$ and $$\mathrm{L}$$ form formed.

(b) In ethanol alkyl chain is present which is responsible for non-polar nature. Water has high value of dipole moment than ethanal. That's why water is more polar than ethanal.

(c) (i) $$\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{I}$$ will react faster than $$\mathrm{CH}_3- \mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{Cl}$$ because $$\mathrm{I}$$ is bigger in size and more polarized atom. Its bond dissociation enthalpy is less So, it easily react with other substances.

(ii) is less reactive when compared to

because in chlorocylo hexane

bond length is less and it is closely attached with cyelohexane while 1-methyl 1-chloro cyclohexane is less stable one extra methyl group is attached which make it more reactive towards $$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{N}} \mathbf{1}$$ mechanism.

OR

(c) (i) 2-Bromo-2-methylbutane > 2-Bromopentane > 1-Bromopentane

(ii) 1-Bromo-3-methyl butane > 2-Bromo-3methyl butane > 2-Bromo-2-methyl butane.
